markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Navigator's Feather](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3607)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Wardens of Wildwood</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=155" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root](/Sources.aspx?ID=242) pg. 79

**Price** 6 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to headgear

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Free Action" /><row>([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612))</row></row>

 

**Trigger** You would roll a [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) check to [Sense Direction](/Actions.aspx?ID=2406) or [Track](/Actions.aspx?ID=2408) but haven’t rolled yet

**Requirement** You’re trained in Survival.
</row>

</column>

---

This jaunty feather is affixed to headgear and can guide you in times of trouble, twisting to point in different directions. When you activate the talisman, you roll the triggering >Survival check to Sense Direction or Track twice, and use the higher result.

For 24 hours, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to Survival checks to Sense Direction, so long as you’re navigating toward the same destination as when you activated the talisman, and a +1 circumstance bonus to Survival checks to Track, so long as you continue to Track the same creature as when you activated the talisman. After 24 hours, the feather disintegrates, becoming mundane dust that blows away on the breeze.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Navigator's Feather

>
[Sage's Bloom](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3608)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Wardens of Wildwood</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=155" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root](/Sources.aspx?ID=242) pg. 79

**Price** 50 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to headgear or hair

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Free Action" /><row>([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))</row></row>

 

**Trigger** You attempt a [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) check to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) but haven’t rolled

**Requirement** You’re trained in Nature.
</row>

</column>

---

This simple white flower is placed into a hat or worn braided into your hair. When activated, it releases a soothing scent, which helps you focus your mind. When you activate the talisman, you Recall Knowledge three times, rather than once. If you use Nature for any of these checks and get a critical failure, that check is instead a failure.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Sage's Bloom

    level="1"
    right="Item 9"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Bloodroot Shield](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3610)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Wardens of Wildwood</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Void" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=510" />
<trait label="Wood" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=508" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root](/Sources.aspx?ID=242) pg. 80

**Price** 600 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Shield** [Wooden Shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=18)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This [wooden shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=18) is an interwoven knot of still-living tree roots ([Hardness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2183) 6, HP 36, BT 18). 

**Activate—Thirsty Roots** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You hold out the shield as the roots untangle themselves and launch forward, dealing 9d6 [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) damage to all creatures in a 60-foot [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388) (DC 25 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2247) Reflex save). On a failed save, a creature takes an additional 5 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86). The shield regains a number of Hit Points equal to half the void damage dealt.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Bloodroot Shield

May contain spoilers from Wardens of Wildwood **Source** [Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root](/Sources.aspx?ID=242) pg. 80 **Price** 5,800 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** 1 --- The _Cythbikian staff_ is a gnarled length of rotting wood, riddled with mold and fungal growths. Originally called the _Zibikian staff_ in honor of its creator, the green man [Zibik](/Deities.aspx?ID=685), with whom Ghorus communed before magically receiving this staff. Ghorus spent years using samples of the staff’s spores to invent weapons for his war against Taldor. When you invest this staff, a thin layer of fungi grows on your hands, remaining until your next daily preparations. If an enemy takes damage from any of the spells you cast by the _Cythbikian staff_, this fungus spreads up your arms, and you gain temporary Hit Points equal to double that spell’s level. These temporary Hit Points last 10 minutes. **Activate—Rule by Roots** ([fungus](/Traits.aspx?ID=614), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per week; **Effect** You plunge the _Cythbikian staff_ into the earth, causing a vast fungal network to spread out from the staff, coating the ground, plants, structures, and all creatures in a 100- foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) (DC 34 Reflex avoids) until the staff is removed or 24 hours have passed. Non-magical plants in the area die. A creature covered by these fungal growths is [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) and [controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=64), physically, by the fungus, as it moves them around like a living puppet. At the beginning of a grabbed creature’s turn, they can attempt to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) (DC 34). On a failure, the fungus forces them to spend all of their remaining actions moving toward and attacking the nearest creature in the area using melee [Strikes](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) with any weapon at their disposal. The fungal growths pay no mind to how the bodies under their control operate, and grabbed creatures are twisted, bent, and contorted in ways that harm their body. Each round, at the end of a grabbed creature’s turn, they take 8d6 bludgeoning damage from this painful manipulation (DC 34 basic Fortitude save). A creature that successfully Escapes or is physically removed from the area is no longer grabbed or controlled. When the _Cythbikian staff_ is removed from the ground, or after 24 hours have passed, the mold dies and this effect ends. Over the course of a week, the mold decays and fertilizes the soil, providing valuable nutrients to the soil and preparing the region for new plant growth. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You expend a number of charges from the staff to cast a spell from its list.
  • **Cantrip** [_ timber_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1412)
  • **1st**[_ wall of shrubs_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1414), [_wooden fists_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1417)
  • **2nd** [_ oaken resilience_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1617), [_splinter volley_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1410)
  • **3rd** [_ sudden blight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2033), [_wall of thorns_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1752)
  • **4th** [_ murderous vine_](/Spells.aspx?ID=953), [_fungal infestation_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1993)
  • **5th** [_ life-draining roots_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1403), [_plant form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1637)
  • **6th** [_ lignify_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1404), [_tangling creepers_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1714)
name: Cythbikian Staff

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Paradise Light](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3615)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Wardens of Wildwood</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Light" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=100" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root](/Sources.aspx?ID=242) pg. 81

**Price** 1,100 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This crystal phial was created from Brilliance, a condensed planar fragment of the plane of [Nirvana](/Planes.aspx?ID=44) which is trapped within Gloaming Arbor. A _paradise light_ glows brightly, shedding bright light in a 30-foot radius and dim light to a further 30 feet. A PC that looks into the paradise light sees images of Nirvana, an idyllic pastoral paradise. A paradise light is required to navigate the heart of the forest in Gloaming Arbor. 

A paradise light automatically attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) any [magical darkness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2404) that it’s light enters. When the paradise light successfully counteracts an area of magical darkness, that area becomes overlain by a reflection of Nirvana, changing the appearance of the area. This illusory transformation lasts for 1 hour.

**Activate—Sanctum** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You conjure a shard of Nirvana in a 30 foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387), temporarily altering the landscape into an soothing meadow for 1 minute. Creatures in the area gain a +1 item bonus to Will saves and Wisdom-based skill checks, and have [fast healing](/Rules.aspx?ID=2322) 2. A creature in the area that attempts to take a [hostile action](/Rules.aspx?ID=2251) must succeed at a DC 28 Will save or the hostile action is prevented and the actions they would’ve spent are wasted.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Paradise Light

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Passage Charm](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3616)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Wardens of Wildwood</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Shadow" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=143" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root](/Sources.aspx?ID=242) pg. 81

**Price** 1,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This glossy black brooch is made of solidified shadows and is a gift, given by Avathrael Realmshaper to their allies to allow them to navigate the heart of the forest within Gloaming Arbor. While wearing a passage charm, you can cast [_darkness_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1480) as an [innate](/Rules.aspx?ID=2232) [occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) spell twice per day.

**Activate—Shape the Shadows** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** You’re in an area that’s [dark](/Rules.aspx?ID=2404) or [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2403); **Effect** You command the shadows to do your bidding, forming a path, a ramp, a wall, or stairs. The _passage charm_ casts 5th-rank [_wall of stone_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1751), except the spell loses the [earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=585) trait, gains the [shadow](/Traits.aspx?ID=693) trait, and has a duration of 24 hours, and the wall is created from solid shadows, rather than stone. If any section of the wall is exposed to [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2402), that portion of the wall has its [Hardness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2183) temporarily reduced by half (to Hardness 7).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Passage Charm

</title>

<traits>

---

This dust is created from the powdered bones of ensnared rabbits and other woodland creatures that died from fright. When inhaled, it pollutes the victim’s mind with fear, awakening an unshakable prey response that sees danger lurking in every shadow.

**Saving Throw** DC 28 Fortitude; **Onset** 1 round; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 4d6 mental damage, [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1, and can’t reduce frightened value for 1 round (1 round); **Stage 2** 4d6 mental damage, frightened 2, and can’t reduce frightened value for 1 round (1 round); **Stage 3** 4d6 mental damage, frightened 2, [fleeing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=74) the poison cloud for 1 round, and can’t reduce frightened value for 1 round (1 round)

</column>
name: Bogeyman Breath

[Reaper's Shadow](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3620)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Wardens of Wildwood</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Injury" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=97" />
<trait label="Poison" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=126" />
<trait label="Virulent" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=162" />
<trait label="Void" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=510" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #203 Shepherd of Decay](/Sources.aspx?ID=243) pg. 76

**Price** 525 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Derived from duskwood sap tapped during the winter solstice, this toxin erodes the connection between body and soul, tricking the latter into assuming the former has already died. Survivors of this near-death experience report ominous tunnel vision, as if the [Grim Reaper](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=104) lurks in their peripheral vision and awaits their final breath.

**Saving Throw** DC 30 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 2d12 [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) damage and [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67) 1 for 1 round (1 round); **Stage 2** 3d12 void damage and doomed 1 for 1 round (1 round); **Stage 3** 3d12 void damage and doomed 1 (1 round); **Stage 4** 3d12 void damage and doomed 2 (1 round)

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Reaper's Shadow

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 13"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Crushing Bough Bracers](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3622)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Wardens of Wildwood</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #203 Shepherd of Decay](/Sources.aspx?ID=243) pg. 76

**Price** 2,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Carved from the heartwood of some impossibly large tree on the [Plane of Wood](/Planes.aspx?ID=25) or [First World](/Planes.aspx?ID=35), crushing bough bracers enable wearers to cling to and shape plant material with ease. The pine cone designs appear to open and bare their seeds when the bracers are in especially verdant areas. 

The bracers grant you a 20-foot [climb Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2349) while climbing [plants](/Traits.aspx?ID=668) or [fungi](/Traits.aspx?ID=614). Your [Strikes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) deal damage to fungus, plant, and wood creatures as though their [resistances](/Rules.aspx?ID=2893) were 5 lower (minimum 0). In addition, the bracers allow you to cast [_timber_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1412) as an innate [primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) cantrip. When you cast _timber_ on a plane with the wood [planar essence trait](/Rules.aspx?ID=3020) (or a supernaturally verdant area, at the GM’s discretion), you can modify the spell in one of the following two ways: increase the spell’s area to 20 feet, to 25 feet if your proficiency rank for spell attack modifier and spell DC is expert, or to 30 feet if your proficiency is legendary; or increase the cantrip’s damage die size to d6.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Crushing Bough Bracers

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Unfolding Tree House](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3626)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Wardens of Wildwood</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Structure" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=153" />
<trait label="Wood" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=508" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #203 Shepherd of Decay](/Sources.aspx?ID=243) pg. 78

**Price** 340 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk**  L (when not activated)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Though it looks like a simple profile of a bird’s wing and body carved into a flat triangle of wood, this magical object can transform into a tree house. 

**Activate—Unfold** <actions string="" /> 1 minute ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You unfold the triangle on near-invisible seams and the bird animates, flying up to perch in a tree you designate within 300 feet of you. The bird’s perch can be as high as 200 feet off the ground or the top of the trees, whichever is lower. The bird then alights, expands, and reshapes into an elegantly crafted tree house over the course of 10 minutes. Unlike typical items with the [structure](/Traits.aspx?ID=704) trait, this tree house attaches to the tree instead of needing to be on solid ground. 

Once the tree house is in place, a ladder made of intricately woven vines attached to the tree house unfurls all the way to the ground. It can be manually pulled up by inhabitants of the tree house for safety. The tree house has a small veranda, circular windows, a door with a simple lock, and six bedrolls inside. Atop a small cushion sits a wooden whistle. 

As an action, you can blow the whistle to create a soft bird call. At this signal, the tree house gradually folds back into a bird over the course of 1 minute, gently ejecting any occupants toward the ladder. Afterward, the bird flies to you, seizes the whistle, and then folds back into the wooden triangle. Unless commanded to dismantle, the tree house stays in place indefinitely, though it’s subject to normal wear and damage.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Unfolding Tree House

>
[Boughshatter](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3627)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Wardens of Wildwood</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Earth" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=55" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Poison" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=126" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #203 Shepherd of Decay](/Sources.aspx?ID=243) pg. 79

**Price** 2,875 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Morningstar](/Weapons.aspx?ID=363)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The spikes of this _[+2](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2830) [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [standard-grade adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2915) [morningstar](/Weapons.aspx?ID=363)_ have a faintly green shimmer, as if resembling Ayrzul’s crystalline teeth. The weapon vibrates briefly when first drawn in or carried into a radioactive area, with the intensity of the vibration correlating to the radioactivity’s strength. While you carry _Boughshatter_, you gain a +4 status bonus to saving throws against radiation, including [Ayrzul’s Blight](/Diseases.aspx?ID=41). 

**Activate—Splintering Strike** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per minute;   **Effect** Make a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) with the morningstar that deals an extra die of weapon damage. If you make this Strike against a [fungus](/Traits.aspx?ID=614), [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=668), or creature with the [wood](/Traits.aspx?ID=508) trait, the Strike ignores 10 points of the target’s resistances to bludgeoning and piercing damage, if any. By damaging such a creature, the morningstar causes the target’s flesh to explode in a shower of splinters that affect a 30-foot cone originating from the target’s space, dealing 8d6 piercing damage to creatures in the area (DC 30 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). You and the target are unaffected by these splinters. 

**Activate—Absorb Corruption** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You take [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) or [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) damage;;  **Effect** You channel some of the toxic energy into _Boughshatter_, granting the weapon the effects of either a [_decaying weapon rune_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2835), grievous weapon rune, or wounding weapon rune for 1 minute. While under this effect, the morningstar glows with [dim green light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2403) in a 20-foot radius.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Boughshatter

May contain spoilers from Curtain Call **Source** [Pathfinder #204: Stage Fright](/Sources.aspx?ID=245) pg. 79 **Price** 3,000 gp **Usage** worn armor **Bulk** L **Base Armor** [Padded Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=40) --- This suit of [padded armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=40) consists of a beautifully woven pleated tunic adorned with a gold pattern and narrow bands of white fur trim on a deep blue background with matching pants, all of which are meant to signal wealth and status. This _[+2](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2785) [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2786) [raiment](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2791) padded armor_ was custom designed for Alessandro Domenesso, an actor as well known for their astounding costume changes as for their roguish off-stage activities. Even without utilizing the magic of this armor, it’s elegant enough that it could easily be mistaken as fine clothing. **Activate—Look At Me!** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Effect** You make yourself visible and apparent to a creature you can see that’s within 30 feet. The _scene stealer’s tunic_ transforms into something incredibly gaudy, distracting, or otherwise compelling to the target creature, who must attempt a DC 30 Will save. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target is distracted by you and keeps looking your way. They take a –1 status penalty to Perception checks against everything but you, a –1 status penalty to all saving throws against your visual effects, and gain a +2 status bonus to all saving throws against visual effects created by anyone else; these penalties and bonus last for 1 minute.
**Failure** As success, but the target is also [off‑guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) to everyone other than you as long as you remain visible.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the target also becomes [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 as they spend extra time on their turn admiring, glowering, or otherwise being distracted by you.
name: Scene Stealer's Tunic

</row>

</column>

---

This stick of dark-gray incense carries a faint, strange odor that’s different for any creature that smells it—mimicking a scent that evokes feelings of nostalgia or homesickness. You begin the activation of a stick of _netherwalk incense_ by lighting it on fire as a single Interact action, at which point you designate up to 10 willing creatures within 20 feet of you. After spending a minute concentrating on the smoke and its scent, you and the affected creatures move into the [Netherworld](/Planes.aspx?ID=36) and can use its warped nature to speed travel. Each hour, your travel covers a distance of 50 miles, during which landmarks appear as vague and symbolic images rather than concrete visuals. You arrive within a mile of your intended destination when you [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) the effect or after 8 hours have passed.

</column>

    level="1"
    right="Item 13"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Mocker's Swazzle](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3630)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Curtain Call</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #204: Stage Fright](/Sources.aspx?ID=245) pg. 80

**Price** 3,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A swazzle is a device made of two strips of metal bound with sinew and around a reed that, when held to the mouth, causes the user’s voice to take on a distinctive rasping tone. Swazzles are often used by street puppeteers to give their puppets’ voices a unique and memorable timber. A _mocker’s swazzle_, though, has a different use—rather than simply giving voices to troublemaking puppets, it helps a performer fight back against hecklers in a crowd.

**Activate—Heckle the Heckler** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** A creature within 30 feet of you attempts and fails to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395) you;  **Effect** You quickly fire a retort back at the triggering creature, mocking its failed attempt in a way that makes it laugh at its own ineptitude; you cast [_laughing fit_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1583) (DC 30 Will save) on the triggering creature. 

**Activate—Mocking Spell** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642), [spellshape](/Traits.aspx?ID=513)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You direct a quick bit of insulting mockery at a creature who can understand you that’s within earshot. If your next action is to cast a [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) spell that targets only that creature, that creature takes a –1 item penalty to any saving throw against the spell and, regardless of the result of the saving throw, becomes [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) until the start of your next turn.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Mocker's Swazzle

[Spectacles of Discernment](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3631)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Curtain Call</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />

**Bulk** L

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

---

Designed for a frequent sponsor of theatrical productions who liked to stay informed of all the off-stage drama, these silver opera glasses are affixed to a slender redwood handle. They can’t be worn on the face but must be raised to the eyes and held there, at which point the spectacles of discernment grant a +2 item bonus to [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2298) checks to notice details at a distance.

**Activate—Reveal the Truth** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You raise the spectacles to regard a target creature through them; the target must be within 60 feet. You immediately gain the ability to speak and understand the language the target creature is currently speaking, or its native language if it’s not currently talking, for 24 hours. During this time, you can Interact with the spectacles of discernment to flip down a pair of supplementary lenses. When you do so, if you’re observing the creature you originally targeted, roll a secret [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=3280) check with a counteract modifier of +23 against any [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629), [morph](/Traits.aspx?ID=657), or [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670) effect affecting the target, but only for the purpose of determining whether you see through the disguise or not.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Spectacles of Discernment

This long, silky neckcloth has the ability to be tied into a convincing semblance of an elegant evening scarf, a worker’s neck kerchief, an elaborate cravat, or any other neckwear. It also provides a boost to vocal performances, enhancing both projection and the emotive qualities of a performance. In its natural state, this item appears as a long, white silk scarf. A _crooner’s cravat_ grants a +1 item bonus to all [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks, but this increases to a +2 item bonus for Performance checks to sing.

**Activate—Adjust Cravat** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You make adjustments to the _crooner’s cravat_, transforming it into any type of stylish chosen neckwear; as you do so, you cast a 1st‑rank [_illusory disguise_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1568) on yourself.

**Activate—Influential Croon** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You pour emotion into your vocal performance, projecting an empathetic bond that fascinates listeners. You cast [_enthrall_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1516) (DC 30 Will save). When you first activate Influential Croon, you can also cast [_subconscious suggestion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1692) on one creature within 30 feet (DC 30 Will save).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Crooner's Cravat

May contain spoilers from Curtain Call **Source** [Pathfinder #204: Stage Fright](/Sources.aspx?ID=245) pg. 80 **Price** 3,000 gp **Usage** worn --- Adorned with golden herald horns, this green ring both enhances a performer’s vocal projection and provides partial protection against sonic attacks. **Activate—Project Voice** ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** When you turn the _herald’s ring_ around your finger three times in either direction, you gain the ability to easily project your voice up to 200 feet without raising the volume of speech, reaching your audience in most venues, including arenas. This effect lasts up to 2 hours. **Activate—Reflect Sound** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You take [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697) damage from a spell or effect; **Effect** You use the _herald’s ring_ to reflect a portion of the sonic damage back at its source by attempting to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) the effect. The ring has a counteract modifier of +23. **Critical Success** All of the damage is reflected back on its source.
**Success** Half of the damage is reflected back at its source, and the other half is negated.
**Failure** Half of the damage is reflected back at the source, and you take the rest of the damage.
**Critical Failure** None of the damage is reflected, but the _herald’s ring_ does absorb enough energy so that you can activate it again today.
name: Herald's Ring

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 13"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Stage Magician's Cloak](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3634)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Curtain Call</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #204: Stage Fright](/Sources.aspx?ID=245) pg. 81

**Price** 3,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn cloak

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This black velvet cloak has a silvery blue iridescent lining. The cloak can be worn with either side facing out—switching from one side to the other requires two Interact actions. If these actions are both taken on the same turn, the act of removing and redonning the cloak doesn’t cause it to lose its [investiture](/Rules.aspx?ID=3163). When worn with the black velvet lining facing out, the _stage magician’s cloak_ grants a +2 item bonus to [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) checks. When worn with the silvery blue lining facing out, it grants a +2 item bonus to [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) checks. 

**Activate—Now You See Me...** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Distracting colorful smoke blasts outward from the cloak in a 10-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387), causing all creatures in the area to become [off‑guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) until the start of your next turn (DC 30 Will save). If you were previously [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62), [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79), or [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=96), you lose those conditions. You become [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=89) for 1 round and may use the extra action to [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297), [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306), or [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305).

**Activate—...Now You Don't** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Harmless silver smoke issues from the cloak in a 10-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387). You cast a 2nd-rank [_invisibility_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577) on yourself and are transported, along with all items you’re wearing and holding, from your current space to an unoccupied space within 30 feet that you can see. If this would bring another creature with you—even if you’re carrying it in an extradimensional space—the transportation part of this effect fails.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Stage Magician's Cloak

    level="1"

    pfs="Limited"
>
[Tumbler's Belt](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3635)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Curtain Call</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #204: Stage Fright](/Sources.aspx?ID=245) pg. 81

**Price** 3,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn belt

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This sparkling purple belt was made for the youngest member of a family of jugglers and tumblers who traveled with the Crascondo Company. The thought was this would allow the child to safely participate with their elder siblings and cousins, but the belt found itself being “loaned” more and more often to dare-taking elders in the troupe. The _tumbler’s belt_ grants a +2 item bonus to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) checks, and whenever you critically succeed at a check to [Tumble Through](/Actions.aspx?ID=2370), you gain a +10-foot item bonus to your [Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2894) until the end of your turn. While wearing the _tumbler’s belt_, you’re not [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) while you [Balance](/Actions.aspx?ID=2369).

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> Land With Grace;  **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You're falling;  **Effect** Treat your fall as if it were 120 feet shorter. Regardless of whether you take damage or not from the fall, you land on your feet.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Tumbler's Belt

    right="Item 7"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Euphorium](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3636)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Curtain Call</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Processed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=487" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #205: Singer, Stalker, Skinsaw Man](/Sources.aspx?ID=246) pg. 81

**Price** 60 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="" /><row>10 minutes ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

An alternative to the magical [_serum of sex shift_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2957), euphorium offers similar benefits with a gustatory twist. Euphorium generally takes the form of a light, fluffy cake decorated with overpoweringly sweet frosting and served with alchemically chilled ice cream.

_Euphorium’s_ effects begin to manifest as you consume it. With every bite of _euphorium_, you become a little more yourself. You can pause at any time, halting the transformation midway at a point you choose. In this case, the results persist for 24 hours before reverting slowly over the course of an additional hour, allowing you to approach the experience at your own pace. 

The remaining dose of _euphorium_ doesn’t spoil (unless it’s temporary due to another effect, such as the limitation of [Quick Alchemy](/Actions.aspx?ID=2801)) but has no effect on anyone other than the person who initially began eating it. This allows you to restart your transformation when you feel comfortable in doing so. Restarting this way quickly restores reverted changes, letting you “pick up where you left off.” Once you consume the final bite, the effect functions as if you’d activated a _serum of sex shift_.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Euphorium

    level="1"
    right="Item 4"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Potion of Passing Fancy](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3637)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Curtain Call</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Potion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=130" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #205: Singer, Stalker, Skinsaw Man](/Sources.aspx?ID=246) pg. 81

**Price** 20 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A lesser variant of the [_serum of sex shift_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2957) with some interesting quirks, a potion of passing fancy is a staple of [Arshean](/Deities.aspx?ID=465) artisans, and most temples keep at least a few doses at hand. Upon drinking the potion, your appearance changes, taking on different sex characteristics in line with another gender expression. You have little control over the details of the change, but it lines up with your deepest heartfelt ideal. More importantly, unlike the _serum of sex shift_, a potion of passing fancy doesn’t impart the “family resemblance” effect; thus, a drinker too shy or afraid of being recognized to publicly express another gender as themself can experience the change more anonymously. 

A _potion of passing fancy’s_ effects last for 1 hour before slowly fading over 10 minutes; drinking another before the effect fully ends resets its duration and undoes any fading. If you extend the effect this way a fourth consecutive time, your actual form stabilizes with your current characteristics while retaining a “family resemblance” to your original form, as per the effects of a single _serum of sex shift._

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Potion of Passing Fancy

May contain spoilers from Curtain Call **Source** [Pathfinder #205: Singer, Stalker, Skinsaw Man](/Sources.aspx?ID=246) pg. 84 **Price** 15,000 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Base Weapon** [Sickle](/Weapons.aspx?ID=364) --- A _sinew-song_ is an ivory violin bow that bears an odd string, one that vibrates on its own, creating the soft sound of violin music whenever it’s swung through the air. When used to play a violin, this ivory bow functions normally but grants a +3 item bonus to any [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) check attempted as a result. It can also be used to mime playing a violin when no instrument is at hand, but in this case, it grants no item bonus to resulting Performance checks. A _sinew-song’s_ primary use is as a weapon—it can be wielded as if it were a _[+3 ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2830) [keen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2843) [ greater thundering](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2850) [ greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [ sickle](/Weapons.aspx?ID=364)_. **Activate—Cutting Cadenza** ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) once and then swipe the weapon through the air three times. Shimmering waves of sound slice out in a 30-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386). All creatures in the area must attempt a DC 37 Reflex save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes damage as if you successfully hit it with a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) using a _sinew-song_, but all of the damage is sonic damage.
**Failure** As success, but the creature also takes 2d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86).
**Critical Failure** As success, but double the damage and the creature takes 4d6 persistent bleed damage.
name: Sinew-Song

May contain spoilers from Curtain Call **Source** [Pathfinder #205: Singer, Stalker, Skinsaw Man](/Sources.aspx?ID=246) pg. 85 **Price** 6,500 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** 1 **Base Weapon** [Morningstar](/Weapons.aspx?ID=363) --- This _[+2 greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [holy](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2842) [shifting](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2846) [morningstar](/Weapons.aspx?ID=363)_ has a head that appears to be a deep crimson metal rose with thorny petals. When you activate a _thorn brush_ to Shift Weapon, you can cause it to take the shape of a painter’s brush. While in this shape, it can’t be used as a weapon (it can still be activated to Shift Weapon back into a morningstar), but it does grant a +2 item bonus to [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) checks to paint or otherwise use the tool while creating artwork. **Activate—Splatter Paint** ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You swing the weapon in an arc, causing a spray of paint of a color of your choice (chosen from black, blue, green, orange, white, or yellow) to splatter all creatures in a 30-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386). Each creature takes 14d6 energy damage based on the color of the paint ([void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) for black, [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) for blue, [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524) for green, [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) for orange, [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509) for white, or [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586) for yellow) and must attempt a DC 34 Reflex save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes half damage.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage and is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) by paint in its eyes for 1 minute.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes double damage and is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) by paint in its eyes for 1 round and then dazzled for 1 minute.
name: Thorn Brush

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 17"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Soulfeeding Mask](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3641)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Curtain Call</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Unholy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=521" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #205: Singer, Stalker, Skinsaw Man](/Sources.aspx?ID=246) pg. 85

**Price** 15,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn mask

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This unsettling mask is made from different flensed faces that have been stitched together with bright red thread. Wearing a _soulfeeding mask_ allows you to see others’ souls within their bodies as long as they’re within 60 feet, as if under the effects of [_see the unseen_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663) and [_truesight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) (but only against living or [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) creatures). Your [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2298) DC increases by 10 against living creatures who [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=2388) undead creatures or against undead creatures who Impersonate living creatures, as you can tell if the creature’s soul has been affected by undeath. The _soulfeeding mask’s_ [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) rank is 9, with a counteract modifier of +31. 

While wearing a _soulfeeding mask_, you gain [darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=2410). 

If you’re [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), you’re [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 2 while you have a _soulfeeding_ mask [invested](/Traits.aspx?ID=637).

**Activate—Devour Soul** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The _soulfeeding mask’s_ eyes and jaw open wide, and clouds of darkness and shadow spill out and attempt to engulf a single creature you can see that’s within 30 feet. That creature takes 50 [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) damage with a DC 36 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2247) Fortitude save. If the target is undead or otherwise has [void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83), this activation loses the death and void traits and gains the vitality trait, and the target takes 50 [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509) damage with a DC 36 basic Fortitude save. If the target takes any damage, you become [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=89) 1 for 1 minute and can use the extra action each round for only [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) actions, unless the damage killed the creature, in which case you can also use the extra action each round for [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) actions as well.

**Activate—Disgorge Soul** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))   **Requirements** You’re quickened as a result of Devour Soul; **Effect** The _soulfeeding mask_ disgorges the devoured soul. You’re no longer quickened from Devour Soul, and the soulfeeding mask casts [_duplicate foe_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1505) (DC 36 Fortitude save) to your specifications, targeting the creature whose soul had been devoured. You must [Sustain this effect](/Actions.aspx?ID=2631), unless the target creature was slain by Devour Soul, in which case duplicate foe functions as if the creature had failed its save, and the _soulfeeding mask_ Sustains this spell automatically.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Soulfeeding Mask

</title>

<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />

---

This luxurious cloak embodies an angel’s swiftness. You gain a +3 item bonus to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) checks and never take [falling](/Rules.aspx?ID=2352) damage, as your cloak billows like a pair of wings to soften any fall you take. When you [invest](/Actions.aspx?ID=2799) in the cloak, you either increase your Dexterity score by 2 or increase it to 18, whichever would give you a higher score. If you are [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521), you are [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 while wearing this cloak.

**Activate—On Angel's Wings** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** The opera cloak transforms into two pairs of brilliant, feathered wings that grant you a [fly Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2350) of 40 feet for 10 minutes. During this time, you gain [immunity](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) to paralysis effects and ignore effects that would give you a circumstance penalty to speed.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

If you are [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521), you become deafened while wearing the toga. 

**Activate—Elysium's Grasp** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** You are exposed to an [inhaled](/Traits.aspx?ID=634) [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669);  **Effect** Purifying gusts of sweet-scented air swirl around you, making you immune to inhaled poisons for 1 minute.

For 8 hours, you become immune to [diseases](/Rules.aspx?ID=2767) spread via inhalation, [olfactory](/Traits.aspx?ID=664) effects, and environmental effects that would prevent you from breathing (including being underwater or from being strangled).

</title>

**Bulk** L

---

This [dragon](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=395)-themed half-mask helps you to navigate through darkness and to notice things from afar—such as helping to find seats in a theater after a show begins, or to pick up details from a show even if you’re seated in the back row! You gain a +3 item bonus to [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2298) checks and never take a penalty to Perception checks based on distance or weather. You gain [darkvision, or greater darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=2410) if you have darkvision. When you [invest](/Actions.aspx?ID=2799) in the mask you either increase your Wisdom score by 2 or increase it to 18, whichever would give you a higher score. If you are [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521), you become [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) as long as you wear the mask.

**Activate—Dragons See the Truth** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** You fail a saving throw against an [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629) effect;  **Effect** The mask attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) the triggering illusion with a counteract rank of 9 and a counteract modifier of +31. You then gain the effects of [_truesight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) for 1 minute.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

>
[Golden Gloves](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3647)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Curtain Call</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Apex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=10" />
<trait label="Holy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=517" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #206: Bring the House Down](/Sources.aspx?ID=247) pg. 82

**Price** 40,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn gloves

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These golden-threaded gloves fit snuggly past the elbows, and imbue all other worn clothing with an aristocratic mien. The gloves grant you a +3 item bonus to [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) skill checks. When you [invest](/Actions.aspx?ID=2799) the gloves, you either increase your Intelligence score by 2 or increase it to 18, whichever would give you a higher score. This gives you additional trained skills and languages, as normal for increasing your Intelligence modifier. You must select skills and languages the first time you invest the gloves, and whenever you invest the same golden gloves, you get the same skills and languages you chose the first time. If you are [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521), you become [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 2 when invested in these gloves.

**Activate—Heaven's Wings** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You throw wide your arms, putting the golden gloves on full display and causing a blazing halo to form above your head. All enemies within a 40-feet [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) must make a DC 41 Fortitude save or be [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) for 1 minute (or [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) for 1 minute on critical failure). [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) creatures take a –2 item penalty to their roll. The halo then melts into your body and attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) any one [affliction](/Rules.aspx?ID=2389) you are currently suffering of your choice with a counteract rank of 9 and a counteract modifier of +31.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Curtain Call</spoilers>

<traits>

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn headwear

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

</column>

---

Lightning crackles from the wings flanking this helm, which is emblazoned with images of valkyries in the service of benevolent deities. You gain a +3 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks and gain [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586) [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) 20. When you [invest](/Actions.aspx?ID=2799) in the helm, you either increase your Strength score by 2 or increase it to 18, whichever would give you a higher score. If you are [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521), you become [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 2 as long as you wear the helm.

**Activate—Path of the Pegasus** <actions string="" /> 10 minutes ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The _sacred valkyrie helm_ casts [_interplanar teleport_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576) to your specifications. When you start this activation, each creature you target with this spell mounts a conjured pegasus who then carries them through reality to arrive at the specified location; each affected creature experiences the 10-minute activation as a kaleidoscopic ride through strange worlds and realities atop a pegasus’s back.

**Activate—Storm's Arms** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The helm casts a 9th-rank [_weapon storm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1758) to your specifications. At your option, all damage caused by this spell is [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586) damage—in this option, the duplicated weapons created by the spell appear to be made of lightning.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

>
[Silvanshee Collar](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3649)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Curtain Call</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Apex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=10" />
<trait label="Holy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=517" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #206: Bring the House Down](/Sources.aspx?ID=247) pg. 82

**Price** 40,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _silvanshee collar_ appears as a collar of prismatic cloth affixed with a tiny bell, but once worn on the body or affixed to your hair, a Tiny [cat](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=376) wearing a similar (but nonmagical) collar appears at your feet. This cat is similar to a [silvanshee](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1055) [agathion](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=216), but its stats are determined as if you gained the [Pet](/Feats.aspx?ID=5186) general feat. When you first [invest](/Actions.aspx?ID=2799) the collar, you must name your silvanshee pet, then build its statistics as detailed in the Pet general feat. The silvanshee has a name (chosen by you) and a unique personality. Whenever you invest the same silvanshee collar, the silvanshee who appears is the same one you named and chose the first time. If you already have a pet, familiar, or other companion that uses the Pet feat, that pet can change its shape as an action to appear as a silvanshee agathion at will (while perhaps retaining some of your pet’s cosmetic traits at your option), but this doesn’t otherwise alter your existing pet’s statistics.

As long as you wear the silvanshee collar, you gain a +3 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks and a +2 circumstance bonus to [Gather Information](/Actions.aspx?ID=2391). When you invest it, you either increase your Charisma score by 2 or increase it to 18, whichever would give you a higher score. If you are [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521), you become [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 2 as long as you wear the collar.

**Activate—Awww!** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** You fail on an attempt to [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=2389) or Make an Impression; **Requirements** Your pet can observe you; **Effect** Your pet does something distracting or adorable at the precise moment you failed, allowing you to reroll the failed check and to use the result of your choice as the actual result.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Silvanshee Collar

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

---

Members of the Empty Hand consider themselves leaders of Belkzen, and their fearsome reputation has helped control Urgir for generations. As a proud member or honored guest of the hold, you’ve been allowed to bear their mark. The tattoo depicts a partially closed hand that grasps nothing. Many people wear this tattoo on their faces, but others have it on an exposed shoulder or arm.

**Activate—Reproach** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You successfully [Coerce](/Actions.aspx?ID=2394) a creature;  **Effect** The maximum duration of the target’s compliance increases to 1d4 days, rather than 1 day.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Empty Hand Marking

</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Triumph of the Tusk</spoilers>

---

Your skin becomes a canvas that records history as you learn it. When you receive the tattoo, choose a [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) skill. You can add the [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727) trait to the [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) action in order to study your tattoos, granting you a +1 item bonus to your check using the chosen Lore skill. A storied skin tattoo starts with an icon that represents a central event in your subject of study and is usually placed on the forehead or over the heart. Each time you learn about a major event in the history of that subject, an image, design, or symbol appears on your skin to represent the event.

**Activate—Living History** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per minute;   **Effect** The tattoo’s design animates for 1 round, crudely portraying some scene associated with the chosen Lore skill.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Storied Skin

The triggering Strike also deals this additional damage.

If the club has a [_greater striking_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) rune, increase the additional damage to 2d6. If the club has a _major striking_ rune, increase the additional damage to 3d6.

May contain spoilers from Triumph of the Tusk **Source** [Pathfinder #208: Hoof, Cinder, and Storm](/Sources.aspx?ID=250) pg. 77 **Price** 135 gp **Usage** worn **Bulk** L --- This stiff, cloth pouch is embroidered with whimsical images of playful pets. When you perform your [daily preparations](/Rules.aspx?ID=2575) in the presence of your creature [companion](/Traits.aspx?ID=558), three treats appear in the bag: an encouraging treat, a reward treat, and a soothing treat. These treats are always suitable for your companion and last until the next time you perform your daily preparations. **Activate—Toss a Treat** ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Trigger** Varies by treat; **Effect** You pull out one of the treats and toss it up to 30 feet into the waiting mouth of your companion. The effect varies by the treat.
  • **Encouraging Treat** ([mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Trigger** You [Command](/Actions.aspx?ID=2400) your companion, and it performs a skill check; **Effect** The treat motivates the companion to perform the task, allowing you to [Aid](/Actions.aspx?ID=2292) it as though you had prepared to help.
  • **Reward Treat** ([healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), mental) **Trigger** Your companion critically succeeds at a skill check or attack roll; **Effect** The treat grants the companion 1d6+6 temporary Hit Points that last until the end of your next turn.
  • **Soothing Treat** (healing) **Trigger** Your companion attempts a save to reduce its [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) condition, would reduce the value of its [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) condition at the end of your turn, or attempts a flat check to end [persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86); **Effect** Reduce the DC of the check to end persistent damage by 2 or reduce the sickened condition by 2. If the companion would reduce its frightened condition value by 1 at the end of its turn, it instead reduces that condition’s value by 2.
**Activate—Rustle the Bag** ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Effect** You [_Command_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1470) your companion. Until the end of your turn, it gains a +5-foot status bonus to each of its Speeds, but must use at least one action to move closer to you.
name: Motivating Treat Bag

</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Triumph of the Tusk</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Contact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=37" />
<trait label="Poison" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=126" />

**Source** [Pathfinder #208: Hoof, Cinder, and Storm](/Sources.aspx?ID=250) pg. 78

**Price** 85 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Distilled from a [trighoul](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3765)’s vital fluids, this gummy, ooze-like toxin springs to life when it makes contact with flesh. It swiftly grows spines that dig into the victim, twitching with rudimentary intelligence as they root around for the nervous system. If the toxin embeds itself deeply enough, it seizes control of the victim’s body.

Unlike most poisons, puppetmaster extract can affect a dead body with mostly intact flesh. Each round, a corpse without sentience automatically fails its Fortitude save against the poison. At stage 4, the poison stops causing damage and confusion; instead, it reanimates the corpse as an [elite plague zombie](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3250) without the unholy trait; a larger body instead uses statistics for a [zombie brute](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3251) or [zombie hulk](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3252), whichever matches its size. The corpse stands as a free action at the beginning of its turn and attacks nearby creatures. When the poison’s duration expires or the poison is cured, the body’s state of animation ends.

**Saving Throw** DC 26 Fortitude; **Onset** 1 minute; **Maximum Duration** 8 rounds; **Stage 1** 1d12 piercing damage and 1d12 poison damage (1 round); **Stage 2** 1d12 piercing damage and 2d12 poison (1 round); **Stage 3** 1d12 piercing damage, 2d12 poison damage, and [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) for 1 round (1 round); **Stage 4** 3d12 poison damage and confused for 1 round (1 round)

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Puppetmaster Extract

With careful alchemical treatments, orc weaponsmiths can enhance the durability and flexibility of giant scorpion tails to create multi-headed _[+1 striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [flails](/Weapons.aspx?ID=374)_. Additional enchantments allow the stingers to generate their own venom. When you would apply this flail’s [critical specialization](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=7), you can instead deal 1d6 [persistent poison damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) to the target. You gain an item bonus to the persistent poison damage equal to the weapon’s item bonus to attack rolls.

**Activate—Envenom** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You temporarily fill the flail’s stingers with venom. The next successful Strike you make with it before the end of your next turn deals 2d6 persistent poison damage to the target. If the Strike critically succeeds and if you would apply persistent poison damage with this weapon’s alternate critical specialization, you combine the two effects to deal 3d6 persistent poison damage to the target.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Venom Lash

</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Triumph of the Tusk</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #208: Hoof, Cinder, and Storm](/Sources.aspx?ID=250) pg. 80

**Price** 80 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

[Aurochs](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3753) meat already makes for tough jerky, but with the right alchemical treatment, it becomes legendarily resilient and imparts that durability to those who eat it. Eating the jerky causes a pair of inch-long horns to grow from your forehead (or causes your existing horns to grow noticeably) for 1 hour. During this time, you gain a +1 item bonus to Fortitude saves. You can end the effect prematurely, causing the horns to retract or revert to their normal size, with a free action that has the concentrate trait.

**Activate—Aurochs' Endurance** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))  **Trigger** You begin your turn and are [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73). ;  **Effect** You suppress the fatigued condition for 10 minutes. **Activate—Shed Cinders** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** You would take fire damage;  **Effect** With a flick of the cloak, you deflect and smother the flames. Until the end of your next turn, your [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333) to remove [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) is 10 instead of 15, which is reduced to 5 if another creature uses a particularly appropriate action to help. If you take at least 5 points of fire damage after applying the fire resistance, the cloak gains the [broken](/Conditions.aspx?ID=60) condition.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Aurochs Hide Armor](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3682)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Triumph of the Tusk</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #209: Destroyer's Doom](/Sources.aspx?ID=251) pg. 82

**Price** 1,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Hide Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=44)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Made of the sturdy, thick hide of the [aurochs](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3753) and tempered to be both flexible and durable, this _[+1 resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2804) [hide armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=44)_ is imbued with the aurochs’ natural defenses against venomous predators. You gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) 5 to poison damage.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Strengthen Resolve;     **Effect** You pull the layers of the armor taut, stiffening your body against incoming forces for 1 minute. While the armor is taut, you have a –1 penalty to Reflex saves and a +2 item bonus to Fortitude saves.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Aurochs Hide Armor

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Bracers of Pain](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3683)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Triumph of the Tusk</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #209: Destroyer's Doom](/Sources.aspx?ID=251) pg. 83

**Price** 2,200 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn bracers

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These simple bracers look plain on the exterior, but a series of small, sharp studs line the interior like rows of shark teeth. While these bracers are worn and [invested](/Traits.aspx?ID=637), you gain a +2 item bonus to Will saves.

**Activate—Sharp Focus** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))  **Trigger** You gain an effect that makes you [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81), [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92), [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94), or [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85);  **Effect** Your bracers snap tight onto your wrists, driving the studs into your skin to shock you into focus. You can attempt to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) the effect causing your condition, with a counteract rank of 6th and a counteract modifier of +22. On a success, you lose the condition. If you have more than one condition from the same source, you only need one counteract check against them.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Bracers of Pain

    level="1"
    right="Item 8"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Gorum's Tear](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3684)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Triumph of the Tusk</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=155" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #209: Destroyer's Doom](/Sources.aspx?ID=251) pg. 83

**Price** 80 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to a weapon

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Free Action" /><row>([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612))</row></row>

 

**Trigger** You make an [attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540) with the affixed weapon.

 
</row>

</column>

---

This teardrop-shaped piece of iron is a naturally occurring mineral, said to be a solidified tear of bellicose joy shed by [Gorum](/Deities.aspx?ID=283) during his battles. When you activate the _Gorum’s tear_, you roll your next attack roll twice and take the better result, ignoring any circumstance penalties. You then become [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) to the creature you targeted until the beginning of your next turn.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Gorum's Tear

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Dirt Sea In A Jar](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3685)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Triumph of the Tusk</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #209: Destroyer's Doom](/Sources.aspx?ID=251) pg. 83

**Price** 880 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This small jar of dark sand swirls intensely as you peer into it, evoking the dangerous beauty of the Dirt Sea.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action or Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You pour the contents of the jar onto unworked ground. If you activate this item with one action, you pour the sand into one or two 5-foot squares adjacent to you. If you activate this item with two actions, the sand spreads across a 15-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386). The affected space turns into [quicksand](/Hazards.aspx?ID=22). Creatures already in the area can Step out of the area as a reaction. The quicksand doesn’t inflict lasting damage to most surfaces or nearby architecture, though a feature surrounded by the quicksand might sink or settle naturally. This terrain lasts for 1 day or until the effect is [Dismissed](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311), causing the sand to reappear in the jar.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Dirt Sea In A Jar

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Needle of Undeath](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3687)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Triumph of the Tusk</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #209: Destroyer's Doom](/Sources.aspx?ID=251) pg. 83

**Price** 1,150 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This thin, wand-like implement is carved from bone and scrimshawed with depictions of battle. While holding the needle of undeath, [mindless](/Traits.aspx?ID=652) [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) creatures see you as one of their own and are [indifferent](/Conditions.aspx?ID=82) to you until you take [hostile actions](/Rules.aspx?ID=2251) against them, and you have a +2 item bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) and [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) checks against intelligent undead. This does not affect a summoned undead’s attitude.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Speak with Undead;  **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You can use Diplomacy to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=2392) on mindless undead or make simple requests of them with a +2 item bonus. You cannot make this request of someone else’s summoned undead.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Needle of Undeath

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 14"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Castrovel's Beacon](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3688)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Spore War</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #210: Whispers in the Dirt](/Sources.aspx?ID=253) pg. 77

**Price** 4,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The tip of this _[+2 greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [brilliant](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2833) standard-grade [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2916) [rapier](/Weapons.aspx?ID=391)_ shines with a flickering sheen of green light that mimics the glittering appearance of Castrovel in the night sky. The carrier of _Castrovel’s Beacon_ instinctively knows Castrovel’s position in the sky even if it hasn’t actually yet risen into view, which grants the wielder a +2 item bonus to [Sense Direction](/Actions.aspx?ID=2406) when using the stars to orient themself. Additionally, the weapon grants a +2 item bonus to all saving throws against effects that cause the [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) or [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) conditions.

When its _brilliant_ rune is activated, the [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) rank is 6 and the counteract modifier is +21.

**Activate—Starlight Burst** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You cast [_vibrant pattern_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1741) from _Castrovel’s Beacon_ as a 6th-rank [arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1) spell with a DC 31 Will save. Creatures with the [elf](/Traits.aspx?ID=588) trait are immune to Starlight Burst, and creatures with the [demon](/Traits.aspx?ID=573) trait take a –2 item penalty on all Will saves against the effect.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Castrovel's Beacon

May contain spoilers from Spore War **Source** [Pathfinder #210: Whispers in the Dirt](/Sources.aspx?ID=253) pg. 77 **Price** 4,500 gp **Usage** held in 2 hands **Bulk** 2 --- The blade of this _[+2 greater striking ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [holy](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2842) standard-grade [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2916) [elven curve blade](/Weapons.aspx?ID=406)_ shines with a pale fire that illuminates the wielder’s face in a grim visage and produces dim light in a 10-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387). The wielder can suppress or reactivate this light as a single action with the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) trait. **Activate—Reveal Demons** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Effect** You brandish _Morning Glow_ and focus on its gleaming light, boosting its illumination so that it produces bright light in a 30-foot emanation (and dim light for another 30 feet) for 10 minutes. All creatures in this area that have the [demon](/Traits.aspx?ID=573) trait must attempt a DC 31 Reflex save. **Success** The demon is unaffected by the glow.
**Failure** The demon becomes outlined with a shimmering aura of dim light that causes it to become dazzled. If the demon was [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=83), it becomes [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) instead. If it was already concealed for any other reason, it is no longer concealed. This light affects the demon for 1 minute.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but with a duration of 10 minutes.
name: Morning Glow
