markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Boots of the Blight](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3693)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Spore War</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #210: Whispers in the Dirt](/Sources.aspx?ID=253) pg. 78

**Price** 1,800 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn shoes

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Tanglebriar is not the only realm plagued by fiendish fungi. The soldiers of Nirmathas have long fought against a similar blight deep in Fangwood, and these boots are one of the more potent tools they’ve developed to help in this pursuit. Now that the Fangwood blight has been mostly contained, many of these boots have been gifted to elven soldiers, for they are equally useful to those who operate within Tanglebriar’s borders. They are also regarded as great trophies for the [demons](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=387) and cultists who dwell within Tanglebriar, for not only can they make use of these boots’ powers, but wearing something created by your enemy gives [Treerazer’s](/Deities.aspx?ID=324) agents yet another way to engage in psychological warfare—they often adorn their boots with severed elf ears, or worse! 

These leather boots remain covered in mud no matter how often they are cleaned. The oiled leather resists water and keeps the feet and legs dry even when wading through water, but the boots retain a damp, fungal smell reminiscent of rotting vegetation. While wearing the boots, you gain a +2 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks to [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=2374) or [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=2381) and to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) checks to [Balance](/Actions.aspx?ID=2369).

**Activate—Swift Sidestep** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** You are about to make a Reflex saving throw against an [environmental hazard](/Rules.aspx?ID=2588) or [terrain feature](/Rules.aspx?ID=1791);  **Effect** The boots help you to avoid the hazard or effect, granting you a +2 status bonus to your saving throw. If you succeed at the saving throw, you become [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=89) for 1 minute, but can use the additional action only to [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305).

**Activate—Fungal Stride** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You ignore the effects of [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) and gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) 10 to damage caused by hazardous terrain. This activation lasts for 10 minutes.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Boots of the Blight

>
[Drumish Pearl Token](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3694)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Spore War</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This silver brooch features an enormous freshwater pearl pulled from the depths of Lake Encarthan. They are often given to trading partners among Drumish merchants as a sign of favor—not as favored as a fellow Kalistocrat, but nonetheless worth treating well. Openly wearing a Drumish pearl token can work wonders when traveling through Druma, whether on legitimate mercantile business or not, but those who flaunt the token inappropriately must beware of repercussions from the Drumish government! The wearer of a Drumish pearl token benefits from a +2 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) checks to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=2392) and to their Perception DC when someone attempts to [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=2389) to them.

**Activate—Subversive Friendship** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You succeed at Making an Impression;  **Effect** You automatically increase the target’s attitude to [helpful](/Conditions.aspx?ID=78). If the target’s initial attitude was [unfriendly](/Conditions.aspx?ID=97) or [hostile](/Conditions.aspx?ID=80), they revert to this attitude after 10 minutes and realize they’ve been manipulated by you. Afterward, they might decide to act against you.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Drumish Pearl Token

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 13"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Pharasmin Visor](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3695)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Spore War</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #210: Whispers in the Dirt](/Sources.aspx?ID=253) pg. 79

**Price** 3,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn headwear

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Popular in Ustalav and other lands where undead are numerous, a [_Pharasmin_](/Deities.aspx?ID=290) _visor_ is a valuable part of any fashionable noble’s armory when attending events in places like Caliphas. This item consists of a metal skullcap helm with a pair of blue crystal lenses extending from each side on metal armatures that can be moved independently in front of or away from the eyes. The helm can appear as either gleaming silver or dull black, and the wearer can alter its appearance whenever they [invest](/Actions.aspx?ID=2799) the visor.

**Activate—Pharasma’s Disdain** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You adjust the visors so that both are lowered. Your vision becomes obscured, causing you to become [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65). At the same time, a tiny fraction of Pharasma’s notice seeps out of the visor to fill the area around you in a 30-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387). All [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) creatures in this area take 12d6 [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) damage (DC 30 basic Will save). At the end of your turn, both visors automatically rise back up, but you remain dazzled until the start of your next turn.

**Activate—Pierce the Night** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))    **Effect** You adjust the helm so that only the left visor is lowered. You gain [darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=2410) until you adjust the visors again or until the item is no longer invested by you, whichever comes first.

**Activate—Reveal the Unseen** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You adjust the helm so that only the right visor is lowered. You can see [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=83) creatures as though they weren’t invisible, although their features are blurred, making them [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) and difficult to identify. You can also see [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=632) creatures like [ghosts](/Traits.aspx?ID=753) when they have phased through an object if you are within 10 feet of an object’s surface; they look like blurry shapes seen through those objects. This effect lasts until 8 hours pass, until you adjust the visors again, or until the item is no longer invested by you, whichever comes first.

**Activate—See Through Death** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))    **Effect** You adjust the visors so that both are raised. The _Pharasmin visor_ grants a +2 item bonus on all saving throws against effects with the [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) trait.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Pharasmin Visor

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Skirmisher's Coat](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3696)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Spore War</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #210: Whispers in the Dirt](/Sources.aspx?ID=253) pg. 79

**Price** 1,300 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn garment

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Valued among soldiers (particularly those from wartorn Nirmathas) who often find themselves operating undercover, this large-collared garment is reminiscent of a highwayman’s coat. A skirmisher’s coat is cleverly constructed, and contains several concealed pockets and grants the wearer a +2 item bonus to checks made to [Conceal an Object](/Actions.aspx?ID=2403).

**Activate—Costume Change** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629))    **Effect** You change the shape and appearance of all your clothing, making them appear as ordinary or fine clothes of your imagining. This doesn’t change the statistics of any armor you’re wearing. Only a creature that’s benefiting from [_truesight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) or a similar effect can attempt to disbelieve this illusion, with a DC of 28.

**Activate—Unexpected Armament** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You reach into one of the coat’s pockets and withdraw a chunk of metal that instantly expands into a common simple or martial melee weapon of your choice. The weapon functions as a _[+1 striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) weapon_. When you produce the weapon, you decide if it is made from [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2916) or [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920), and which one of the following property runes it gains: [corrosive](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2834), [flaming](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2838), [frost](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2839), [shock](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2847), or [thundering](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2850). This weapon remains for 1 hour or until it leaves your grasp, at which point it vanishes.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Skirmisher's Coat

    level="1"
    right="Item 15"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Anima Robe](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3697)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Spore War</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #211: The Secret of Deathstalk Tower](/Sources.aspx?ID=254) pg. 81

**Price** 6,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn garment

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This robe was the favored garment of the legendary Ekujae hero, Iyalirrin. When the draconic god [Dahak](/Deities.aspx?ID=150) threatened destruction, Iyalirrin was the primary architect of a powerful ritual to banish him called the anima invocation. Iyalirrin and many others were forced to sacrifice themselves to empower this ritual, but he ensured his _Anima Robe_ remained in good hands before he did so. Since then, the Anima Robe has been worn by dozens of elven occultists and [bards](/Classes.aspx?ID=32) who have traveled Golarion and beyond. The _Anima Robe_ has remained in the care of Queen Telandia for only the past few dozen years.

While wearing the Anima Robe, you gain a +2 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) checks made to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=2392) and to all [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks. You also gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) 10 to to mental damage.

**Activate—Who Am I?** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727))    **Effect** With a toss of the robe’s hood, you cast a 3rd-rank [_illusory disguise_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1568) on yourself.

**Activate—Who Are We?** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** With a whirl of the robe’s hem, you cast a 7th-rank [_illusory disguise_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1568).

**Activate—Who Are They?** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [olfactory](/Traits.aspx?ID=664), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** With a swish of the robe’s sleeve, you cast a 7th-rank [_illusory creature_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1567).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Anima Robe

May contain spoilers from Spore War **Source** [Pathfinder #211: The Secret of Deathstalk Tower](/Sources.aspx?ID=254) pg. 81 **Price** 6,500 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** 1 --- After she led her people through the Darklands to the far side of the world, the elven oracle Jininsiel established the nation of Jinin in the heart of the continent of Tian Xia. As she sought to forge alliances with other lands surrounding her own, Jininsiel crafted potent magic items as gifts. For the people of Tianjing, she created this staff, which served the people of that nation well for many years. They returned it to Jinin as a token of condolence when that nation’s leader passed into the Great Beyond. Many centuries later, when the people of Jinin learned that their kin had returned to Kyonin from Castrovel only to face fiendish threats in their homeland, a group of priests from Jinin traveled across the world to help. They brought with them _Fiendbreaker_ and chose to leave it in Kyonin to help protect them in the future from [demonic](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=387) foes. _Fiendbreaker_ functions as a _[+2 greater striking ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [holy](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2842) standard-grade [cold iron ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2916) [staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=367)_. While wielding the staff, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to checks made to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) about [fiends](/Traits.aspx?ID=600). **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You expend a number of charges from the staff to cast a spell from its list.
  • **Cantrip** [_divine lance_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1498)
  • **1st** [_sanctuary_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1660)
  • **2nd** [see the unseen](/Feats.aspx?ID=2815)
  • **3rd** [_anointed ground_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1965), [_holy light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1557)
  • **4th** [_clear mind _](/Spells.aspx?ID=1469), [_planar tether_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1636)
  • **5th** [_banishment _](/Spells.aspx?ID=1448), [_divine wrath _](/Spells.aspx?ID=1499)
  • **6th** _holy light_, [_spirit blast_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1685)

>
[Guiding Star Orb](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3699)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Spore War</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #211: The Secret of Deathstalk Tower](/Sources.aspx?ID=254) pg. 81

**Price** 6,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Candlaron the Sculptor is one of Kyonin’s most storied and honored wizards. While his final fate is unknown, his greatest creations, the aiudara, remain behind as a legacy of his power—as do other potent items, such as the _Guiding Star Orb_, a navigational traveling aid that the heroic wizard often relied upon when venturing into an unexplored part of the world.

**Activate—Embed Location** <actions string="" /> 10 minutes ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))    **Effect** By focusing on the _Guiding Star Orb_, you embed your current location in the item. Thereafter, anyone who holds the _Guiding Star Orb_ while [Casting a Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734) with the [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710) trait to travel to this location arrives precisely, without any inaccuracy at all. The _Guiding Star Orb_ can only have one location embedded at a time; if you use this activity a second time, the new location replaces the previous one.

**Activate—Momentary Aiudara** <actions string="" /> 10 minutes ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You cause a shimmering magical archway to appear next to you as the _Guiding Star Orb_ casts a 7th-rank [_teleport_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1720) to your specifications. If you are teleporting to an aiudara you’ve visited before, you and the targets appear precisely at that location.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Guiding Star Orb

>
[Slithermaw's Bane](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3700)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Spore War</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #211: The Secret of Deathstalk Tower](/Sources.aspx?ID=254) pg. 82

**Price** 6,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Chain Shirt](/Armor.aspx?ID=6)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This suit of _[+2 greater resilient ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2804) [elven chain](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2801)_ was worn by the elven hero Kyloss Syndar. The armor served him well, but eventually he fell in battle against the demonic hydra Slithermaw. As Kyloss slew the demonic beast, its fangs pierced his chest and mortally wounded the elf. The armor retains several ragged holes along the chest and abdomen where the hydra’s teeth damaged it. 

_Slithermaw’s Bane_ grants its wearer poison [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) 10, and the [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2786) rune increases the item bonus on saving throws versus [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) by 1 (to +3).

**Activate—Calistria’s Sting** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** A creature [grapples](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376) you;  **Effect** Poison wells up from the armor’s links to seep into the triggering creature’s body, causing it to suffer wracking pains as if it were being stung by thousands of angry wasps. The triggering creature takes 7d6 [persistent poison damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) (DC 34 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2247) Fortitude save); this persistent damage cannot be ended as long as the triggering creature continues to grapple you.

**Activate—Terrain Adaptation** <actions string="" /> 10 minutes ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You alter the exterior of the armor to better adapt to the surrounding [terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=3108): [aquatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2770), [arctic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2773), [desert](/Rules.aspx?ID=2776), [forest](/Rules.aspx?ID=2779), [mountain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2783), [plains](/Rules.aspx?ID=2789), sky, [swamp](/Rules.aspx?ID=2792), or [underground](/Rules.aspx?ID=2808). You ignore non-magical [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) within the chosen environment and gain a +1 circumstance bonus to saving throws against [environmental hazards](/Rules.aspx?ID=2588), [natural disasters](/Rules.aspx?ID=2823), and [extreme temperatures](/Rules.aspx?ID=2820) that originate from that terrain. You are also protected from severe and extreme heat or severe and extreme cold (your choice when you activate this ability). This effect lasts until your next [daily preparation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2575).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Slithermaw's Bane

>
[Soulcutter](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3701)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Spore War</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #211: The Secret of Deathstalk Tower](/Sources.aspx?ID=254) pg. 82

**Price** 6,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Elven Curve Blade](/Weapons.aspx?ID=406)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This _[+2 greater striking ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [astral ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2832) [elven curve blade](/Weapons.aspx?ID=406)_ was the treasured weapon of the [Calistrian](/Deities.aspx?ID=279) witch Silisifex, who played several key roles in reclaiming Kyonin from Tanglebriar when the elves returned to Golarion. Although a witch, her mesmerizing skill with the curved blade rivaled that of many soldiers. Her reasons for leaving Soulcutter behind in Kyonin before her final mission into Tanglebriar in an attempt to purify Deathstalk Tower are unknown.

As long as you carry _Soulcutter_, you gain its potency bonus as an item bonus to all saving throws against [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) effects. This bonus increases by 2 against [possession](/Traits.aspx?ID=671) effects.

**Activate—Soothe Souls** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You whirl _Soulcutter_ in the air around you, rejuvenating the living within a 20-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) around you while castigating those in that area who have no place in nature. You can [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) this activation for up to 1 minute. Living creatures that start their turn in the area regain 1d8 Hit Points, and any [fiend](/Traits.aspx?ID=600) or [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) creature that starts its turn in the area takes 1d8 [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) damage.

**Activate—Soulcutting Storm** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You swing _Soulcutter_ and cast a 7th-rank [_weapon storm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1758) to your specifications, but all damage caused by the spell is spirit damage. If used to damage a creature that’s possessing another creature, this spell does no damage to the possessed creature.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Soulcutter

markdown:
May contain spoilers from Spore War **Source** [Pathfinder #211: The Secret of Deathstalk Tower](/Sources.aspx?ID=254) pg. 82 **Price** 6,500 gp **Usage** held in 1+ hand(s) **Bulk** 1 **Base Weapon** [Composite Shortbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=435) --- This _[+2 greater striking ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [frost ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2839) [composite shortbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=435)_ belonged to a legendary scout, Jelarial, who found herself in command of a company of elves fleeing Mierani north into the Crown of the World to escape the doom of Earthfall thousands of years ago. Wintershot resurfaced during the war to reclaim Kyonin from Tanglebriar several thousand years later, wielded by a succession of mysterious snipers whose movements through the woodland confounded the demons, so much so that it gave rise to rumors that Jelarial’s ghost had returned to the south to aid her kin in a time of need. When making a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) with _Wintershot_, targets do not gain [concealment](/Rules.aspx?ID=424) from the effects of mist or precipitation, and circumstance penalties to attacks of up to –2 imparted from [strong winds](/Rules.aspx?ID=2821) are negated. **Activate—Auroral Shine** ([cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) **Frequency** once per 10 minutes; **Effect** Fire an arrow at a target. If you hit, instead of dealing damage with the arrow, the creature struck must attempt a DC 34 Fortitude save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is surrounded by shimmering lights akin to an aurora, causing it to become [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) for 2 rounds. If the creature was [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=83), it becomes [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) instead. If the creature was already concealed for any other reason, it is no longer concealed.
**Failure** As success, but the creature also takes 2d6 [persistent cold damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), and the light affects the creature for 1 minute.
**Critical Failure** As success, but the creature also takes 4d6 persistent cold damage, and the light affects the creature for 10 minutes. **Activate—Signal Flare** ([light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You pull back _Wintershot’s_ string and fire an arrow straight upward. The arrow soars to a height of 500 feet, or until it strikes a solid surface like a ceiling. When it reaches its apex, it explodes in a brilliant burst, creating a 100-foot radius area of bright light and dim light in the next 100 feet. In the night sky, this beacon can be seen clearly for miles. The beacon remains lit for up to 1 minute in a color of your choice. Alternately, you can fire a Signal Flare like a normal arrow to attempt to strike a Target—if it hits, the arrow inflicts normal damage and attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) one darkness effect of your choice that affects the area you hit with a counteract rank of 7th and a counteract modifier of +26.
name: Wintershot

Deathstalk mushrooms that have been alchemically treated into this poison cause those who succumb to suffer horrific hallucinations in which everyone around them distorts into demonic shapes shortly before their own bodies begin to break down and melt from within. Creatures with the fungus trait are immune to this poison and often find the flavor of a deathstalk mushroom to be rather pleasant. 

**Saving Throw** DC 35 Fortitude; **Onset** 1 minute; **Maximum Duration** 6 minutes; **Stage 1** [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 (1 minute); **Stage 2** [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) and stupefied 2 (1 minute); **Stage 3** 16d6 poison damage, confused, and stupefied 3 (1 minute); **Stage 4** 17d6 poison damage, confused, and stupefied 4 (1 minute)

</column>

The kora grants a +2 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks made to tell stories when used to accompany song or speech. _The kora of the unending story_ is a coda instrument—an item that functions like a [staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=367) but in the form of a [musical instrument](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2737). **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You expend a number of charges from the kora to cast a spell from its list.

    level="1"
    right="Item 14"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[What Doors We Open](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3705)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Spore War</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Grimoire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=387" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #211: The Secret of Deathstalk Tower](/Sources.aspx?ID=254) pg. 83

**Price** 4,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_What Doors We Open_ is the culmination of Aravashnial’s research. Before he was captured, he performed a 1-minute ritual to erase his spells from this book and then hid it at his campsite to prevent his [demonic](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=387) foes from learning too many of his secrets. A spellcaster can transfer their own spells to this grimoire using a similar 1-minute ritual. When a spellcaster prepares their spells from it, they gain the ability to Activate the grimoire until their next [daily preparations](/Rules.aspx?ID=2575) and gain a +2 item bonus to checks made to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) about demons. A grimoire’s benefits apply only to spells cast via spell slots. No one can use more than one grimoire per day, nor can a grimoire be used by more than one person per day.

**Activate—Cast Out** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** If the next activity you take this round is to cast [_banishment_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1448) prepared from this grimoire, it counts as the extra cost of adding an object that is anathema to the creature and gives the creature a –2 circumstance penalty to its save. If the targeted creature is a [demon](/Traits.aspx?ID=573), increase this to a –3 circumstance penalty.

**Activate—Open the Door Within** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))    **Effect** The inside back cover of _What Doors We Open_ features a realistic depiction of a bejeweled door set in a stone wall. When you Open the Door Within, you open that door to reveal an extradimensional space that otherwise functions identically to a [type II spacious pouch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3032).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: What Doors We Open

May contain spoilers from Spore War **Source** [Pathfinder #212: A Voice in the Blight](/Sources.aspx?ID=255) pg. 79 **Price** 60,000 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Base Weapon** [Dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=358) --- This slim _[+3 major striking ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [unholy ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2851) [wounding ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2854) [dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=358)_ has a curved blade akin to that of a peeling knife. Soft elf-leather strips adorn the handle. If you are a demon, it amplifies any auras you have by granting a +1 item bonus to your aura’s DCs. **Activate—Burrowing Splinter** ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day; **Requirements** Your previous action was a successful [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) with the _apotheosis knife_; **Effect** You twist the blade, causing a sliver of the dagger to break off inside the body of the creature you damaged with the apotheosis knife on your prior action. Just as the blade itself cuts away flesh, this splinter slices away at the target’s mind. The target must attempt a DC 43 Will save. If the target is a [demon](/Traits.aspx?ID=573), the burrowing splinter automatically triggers the effects of their sin vulnerability, regardless of their saving throw result. **Critical Success** The target takes no additional damage.
**Success** The target takes 6d6 [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) damage and is [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 until the end of their next turn.
**Failure** The target takes 6d6 [persistent mental damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) and is stupefied 1 with an unlimited duration.
**Critical Failure** The target takes 12d6 persistent mental damage, is stupefied 1 with an unlimited duration, and is [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 3.
name: Apotheosis Knife

May contain spoilers from Spore War **Source** [Pathfinder #212: A Voice in the Blight](/Sources.aspx?ID=255) pg. 79 **Price** 12,500 gp **Usage** held in 2 hands --- _Void shackles_ are built from supernatural crystals that form in the Void—materials that dig painfully into the flesh of those they restrain. A set of void shackles functions as [superior manacles](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2733), but while they are in the Void, the crystals fluctuate in response to the plane’s energy, which increases all DCs to remove them or [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) by 1 to DC 43. If a check to remove void shackles from a creature fails, the wearer of the manacles takes 10d6 [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) damage (DC 37 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save). While a creature is manacled, the void shackles attempt to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) any [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710) effect that targets the manacled creature, with a counteract rank of 9th and a +25 modifier to the roll. If the teleportation effect is countered, the manacled creature takes 10d6 void damage (DC 37 basic Fortitude save). _Void shackles_ can be placed on [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=632) creatures as if they were not incorporeal. An incorporeal creature manacled by _void shackles_ cannot pass through solid objects. Even a soul can be restrained via _void shackles_, provided the soul was of a 17th- or lower-level creature in life. A soul held in _void shackles_ cannot move on to the afterlife, cannot become [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722), and can be transported by simply transporting the shackles. **Activate—Affix Shackle** ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Effect** You affix the _void shackles_ to a helpless or willing creature or to any 17th- or lower-level soul. The shackles grow in size to accommodate the wrists or ankles of any size creature. **Activate—Release Shackle** ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Effect** You cause the void shackles to open and release their prisoner. If you were not the one to affix the shackles to that creature or soul, you must attempt a DC 43 Will save. **Critical Success** The shackles open.
**Success** The shackles open, but you and the shackled creature take 5d6 [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) damage.
**Failure** The shackles do not open, and you and the shackled creature take 10d6 void damage.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but 20d6 void damage.
name: Void Shackles

This device resembles a teapot with a trigger. When filled with cloth fuel (worth 1 cp) and lit, it smolders and gradually fills with smoke for 10 minutes, during which time you can squeeze the trigger to spray smoke. 

**Activate—Spray Smoke** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per minute;   **Effect** You expel smoke into an adjacent 5-foot square. All creatures in that area are [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62), and other creatures are concealed to them. The smoke lasts for 1d4 rounds or until dispersed by strong [wind](/Rules.aspx?ID=643).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Beekeeper's Smoker

[Creeping Death](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3717)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Wardens of Wildwood</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Contact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=37" />
<trait label="Poison" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=126" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root](/Sources.aspx?ID=242) pg. 17

**Price** 45 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

**Saving Throw** DC 22 Fortitude; **Onset** 1 round; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 2d6 poison damage and [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 1 (2 rounds); **Stage 2** 2d6 poison damage and [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) (1 round); **Stage 3** 2d6 poison damage and [controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=64) (2 days); Stage 4 dead. A creature that dies while infected with creeping death immediately releases a burst of spores in a 15-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387), exposing creatures in the area to creeping death. If the corpse of a creature killed by creeping death isn’t burned, it rises as the host of a cythnophorian 12 hours later.

</column>

<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=89" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Vitality" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=509" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #201: Pactbreaker](/Sources.aspx?ID=241) pg. 81

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This gnarled wand is made from the branch of an [arboreal](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=364). 

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554) at the indicated rank. After you cast the spell, the raw primal energy stored within the arboreal from which the wand was made washes over one target you choose and tries to purify its essence. The wand attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) the lowest level [affliction](/Rules.aspx?ID=2389) affecting your target. It uses your casting modifier and a counteract rank of half the wand’s item level rounded up.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of _heal_ of the appropriate level and a branch freely given by an arboreal.

    level="1"

[Arboreal Wand](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3718)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Wardens of Wildwood</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=89" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This gnarled wand is made from the branch of an [arboreal](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=364). 

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554) at the indicated rank. After you cast the spell, the raw primal energy stored within the arboreal from which the wand was made washes over one target you choose and tries to purify its essence. The wand attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) the lowest level [affliction](/Rules.aspx?ID=2389) affecting your target. It uses your casting modifier and a counteract rank of half the wand’s item level rounded up.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of _heal_ of the appropriate level and a branch freely given by an arboreal.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6+"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Skitter Knot](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3719)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Wardens of Wildwood</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #201: Pactbreaker](/Sources.aspx?ID=241) pg. 81

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Free Action" /><row>envision</row></row>

 

**Trigger** You are [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=69) at the beginning of your turn.

 
</row>

</column>

---

Carved from an [arboreal’s](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=364) knuckle, this wooden torus can be activated despite your being unconscious. The knot then sprouts several roots that arch over your body like a spider’s legs. These lift you a few inches off the ground before [Striding](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) and carrying you with them. The roots prioritize taking you away from obvious harm, though as a reaction, an ally who speaks [Arboreal](/Languages.aspx?ID=26) can command the roots to carry you to a particular point within range. 

After moving, the roots continue protecting you; this counts as [Raising a Shield](/Actions.aspx?ID=2316), giving you a +1 circumstance bonus to AC until the beginning of your next turn. The roots use the [Shield Block](/Feats.aspx?ID=5212) reaction against the first physical attack against you, after which the roots crumble.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 6"
pfs="Limited"
>
Lesser Skitter Knot
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #201: Pactbreaker](/Sources.aspx?ID=241) pg. 81

**Price** 50 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The roots carry you up to 10 feet and have [Hardness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2183) 5.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Limited"
>
Greater Skitter Knot
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #201: Pactbreaker](/Sources.aspx?ID=241) pg. 81

**Price** 275 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The roots carry you up to 15 feet and have Hardness 10.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 16"
pfs="Limited"
>
Major Skitter Knot
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #201: Pactbreaker](/Sources.aspx?ID=241) pg. 81

**Price** 1,800 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The roots carry you up to 20 feet and have Hardness 20.
name: Skitter Knot

The roots prioritize taking you away from obvious harm, though as a reaction, an ally who speaks [Arboreal](/Languages.aspx?ID=26) can command the roots to carry you to a particular point within range. 

After moving, the roots continue protecting you; this counts as [Raising a Shield](/Actions.aspx?ID=2316), giving you a +1 circumstance bonus to AC until the beginning of your next turn. The roots use the [Shield Block](/Feats.aspx?ID=5212) reaction against the first physical attack against you, after which the roots crumble.

</column>

**Base Armor** [Full Plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=50)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Golden images of heroic deeds decorate the black plates of this _[+1](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2785) [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2786) [full plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=50)_. You gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) 5 to [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) damage and a +1 status bonus to saves against [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598). 

**Activate—Personal Legend** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The scenes depicted on your armor shift and change, making room to accommodate your current heroic feats. You cast [_heroism_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1555) on yourself.

    level="1"
    right="Item 2+"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Defoliation Bomb](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3721)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Wardens of Wildwood</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Bomb" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=21" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Splash" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=150" />
<trait label="Void" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=510" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root](/Sources.aspx?ID=242) pg. 77

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Strike</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This brightly painted ceramic sphere contains chemicals that cause plants to wither and die. A defoliation bomb deals the listed [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) damage, [persistent void damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), and [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage to all plants in the area. Non-creature [plants](/Traits.aspx?ID=668) in the area immediately wither and die. Many types grant a bonus to attack rolls.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 2"
pfs="Limited"
>
Lesser Defoliation Bomb
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root](/Sources.aspx?ID=242) pg. 77

**Price** 7 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The bomb deals 1d6 void damage, 1d4 persistent void damage, and 1 void splash damage.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Limited"
>
Moderate Defoliation Bomb
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root](/Sources.aspx?ID=242) pg. 78

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You gain a +1 item bonus to attack rolls. The bomb deals 2d6 void damage, 2d4 persistent void damage, and 2 void splash damage.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 12"
pfs="Limited"
>
Greater Defoliation Bomb
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root](/Sources.aspx?ID=242) pg. 78

**Price** 35 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You gain a +2 item bonus to attack rolls. The bomb deals 3d6 void damage, 3d4 persistent void damage, and 3 void splash damage.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 18"
pfs="Limited"
>
Major Defoliation Bomb
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2+"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Defoliation Bomb](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3721)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Wardens of Wildwood</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Bomb" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=21" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Splash" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=150" />
<trait label="Void" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=510" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root](/Sources.aspx?ID=242) pg. 77

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Strike</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This brightly painted ceramic sphere contains chemicals that cause plants to wither and die. A defoliation bomb deals the listed [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) damage, [persistent void damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), and [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage to all plants in the area. Non-creature [plants](/Traits.aspx?ID=668) in the area immediately wither and die. Many types grant a bonus to attack rolls.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

pfs="Limited"

</title>

**Price** 7 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The bomb deals 1d6 void damage, 1d4 persistent void damage, and 1 void splash damage.

pfs="Limited"
>
Moderate Defoliation Bomb
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

</column>

---

level="2"
right="Item 12"
pfs="Limited"
>
Greater Defoliation Bomb
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root](/Sources.aspx?ID=242) pg. 78

**Price** 35 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You gain a +2 item bonus to attack rolls. The bomb deals 3d6 void damage, 3d4 persistent void damage, and 3 void splash damage.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 18"
pfs="Limited"
>
Major Defoliation Bomb
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root](/Sources.aspx?ID=242) pg. 78

**Price** 3,600 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You gain a +3 item bonus to attack rolls. The bomb deals 4d6 void damage, 4d4 persistent void damage, and 4 void splash damage.
name: Greater Defoliation Bomb

<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Strike</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

---

This pressurized iron casing bursts open when struck, releasing [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2916) shrapnel. The bomb deals the listed piercing damage and piercing [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage from a cold iron source. Many types grant a bonus to attack rolls.

</column>

right="Item 2"
pfs="Limited"

</title>

</traits>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The bomb deals 2d4 piercing damage and 1 piercing splash damage.

right="Item 4"
pfs="Limited"
>
Moderate Nail Bomb
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

pfs="Limited"
>
Greater Nail Bomb
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root](/Sources.aspx?ID=242) pg. 78

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This pressurized iron casing bursts open when struck, releasing [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2916) shrapnel. The bomb deals the listed piercing damage and piercing [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage from a cold iron source. Many types grant a bonus to attack rolls.

</row>

---

This pink flower has long, slender thorns along the stem. The flower can be used as a [catalyst](/Rules.aspx?ID=1509) when casting an [_entangling flora_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1515) spell, which causes the affected plants to sprout long thorns and vibrant pink blossoms. The area becomes [hazardous terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2368), dealing the listed piercing damage to an enemy each time it enters an affected square.

<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root](/Sources.aspx?ID=242) pg. 78

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><row>[Cast a Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734) (add 1 action)</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This wooden whistle is stuffed with cotton and carved with images of bellowing stags, howling wolves, and chirping birds. You can blow on this whistle to use it as a catalyst when casting an [_animal form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1440) spell. When you do, you gain the listed benefit for the duration of the spell.

markdown:
May contain spoilers from Wardens of Wildwood **Source** [Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root](/Sources.aspx?ID=242) pg. 78 **Usage** affixed to armor or a weapon **Bulk** L --- This small piece of wood is finely carved to depict a foxglove. The spell DC of any spell cast by [Activating](/Rules.aspx?ID=1489) this item is 17.
  • **Armor** You gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) 2 to poison damage.
  • **Weapon** After you cast a [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=668) spell by activating the foxglove token, your Strikes with the weapon it's attached to deal an additional 1d4 poison damage until the end of your next turn.
**Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You cast [_puff of poison_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2016). Foxglove Token **Source** [Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root](/Sources.aspx?ID=242) pg. 78 **Price** 55 gp Greater Foxglove Token **Source** [Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root](/Sources.aspx?ID=242) pg. 78 **Price** 425 gp --- Resistance when affixed to armor is 5, extra damage when affixed to a weapon is 1d6, and the spell DC is 24. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast 3rd-rank [_noxious vapors_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2011). Major Foxglove Token **Source** [Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root](/Sources.aspx?ID=242) pg. 78 **Price** 1,750 gp --- Resistance when affixed to armor is 10, extra damage when affixed to a weapon is 1d8, and the spell DC is 29. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_petal storm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=967). **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast 4th-rank [_wall of thorns_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1752).
name: Foxglove Token

May contain spoilers from Wardens of Wildwood **Source** [Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root](/Sources.aspx?ID=242) pg. 79 **Usage** affixed to armor or a weapon **Bulk** L --- This burgundy moss grows in heart-shaped clumps and releases a pleasant, calming scent. The spell DC of any spell cast by Activating this item is 17.
  • **Armor** You gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) 2 to [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) damage and a +1 item bonus to saving throws against effects with the [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590) trait.
  • **Weapon** After you cast a [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623) spell by activating the _heartmoss_, the weapon exudes a soothing scent. Your [Strikes](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) with the affixed weapon have the [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661) trait for 1 minute. Until the end of your next turn, Strikes with the affixed weapon cause the target to be [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 for 1 round if it hits (or for 3 rounds on a critical hit).
**Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You cast [_stabilize_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1689). Heartmoss **Source** [Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root](/Sources.aspx?ID=242) pg. 79 **Price** 55 gp Greater Heartmoss **Source** [Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root](/Sources.aspx?ID=242) pg. 79 **Price** 450 gp --- Resistance when affixed to armor is 5, stupefied value when affixed to a weapon is 2, and the spell DC is 24. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast 3rd-rank [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554). Major Heartmoss **Source** [Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root](/Sources.aspx?ID=242) pg. 79 **Price** 1,750 gp --- Resistance when affixed to armor is 10, stupefied value when affixed to a weapon is 3, and the spell DC is 29. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast 4th-rank [_sound body_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1679). **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_healing well_](/Spells.aspx?ID=926).
name: Heartmoss

markdown:
May contain spoilers from Wardens of Wildwood **Source** [Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root](/Sources.aspx?ID=242) pg. 79 **Usage** affixed to armor or a weapon **Bulk** L --- This burgundy moss grows in heart-shaped clumps and releases a pleasant, calming scent. The spell DC of any spell cast by Activating this item is 17.
  • **Armor** You gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) 2 to [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) damage and a +1 item bonus to saving throws against effects with the [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590) trait.
  • **Weapon** After you cast a [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623) spell by activating the _heartmoss_, the weapon exudes a soothing scent. Your [Strikes](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) with the affixed weapon have the [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661) trait for 1 minute. Until the end of your next turn, Strikes with the affixed weapon cause the target to be [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 for 1 round if it hits (or for 3 rounds on a critical hit).
**Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You cast [_stabilize_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1689). Heartmoss **Source** [Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root](/Sources.aspx?ID=242) pg. 79 **Price** 55 gp Greater Heartmoss **Source** [Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root](/Sources.aspx?ID=242) pg. 79 **Price** 450 gp --- Resistance when affixed to armor is 5, stupefied value when affixed to a weapon is 2, and the spell DC is 24. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast 3rd-rank [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554). Major Heartmoss **Source** [Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root](/Sources.aspx?ID=242) pg. 79 **Price** 1,750 gp --- Resistance when affixed to armor is 10, stupefied value when affixed to a weapon is 3, and the spell DC is 29. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast 4th-rank [_sound body_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1679). **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_healing well_](/Spells.aspx?ID=926).
name: Greater Heartmoss

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6+"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Wand of Splintered Sorrows](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3727)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Wardens of Wildwood</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
<trait label="Wood" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=508" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root](/Sources.aspx?ID=242) pg. 79

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This wooden wand is roughly cut, as if it had been crudely chopped from a tree and left forgotten. When held, the wand imparts feelings of deep sorrow. 

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_splinter volley_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1410) of the indicated rank. Each splinter contains some of the despair felt by cruelly harvested trees, causing any creature damaged by this spell to become stupefied 2 for a number of rounds equal to the spell rank. On a critical success on the attack roll, the target also weeps, becoming [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 for the same duration.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of _splinter volley_ of the appropriate level.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

markdown:
May contain spoilers from Wardens of Wildwood **Source** [Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root](/Sources.aspx?ID=242) pg. 80 **Usage** affixed to armor or a weapon --- This dry clay ball becomes malleable when activated, shifting into a variety of forms. The spell attack roll of any spell cast by [Activating](/Rules.aspx?ID=1489) this item is +7 and the spell DC 17.
  • **Armor** You gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) 2 to precision damage and a +1 item bonus to saving throws against effects with the [morph](/Traits.aspx?ID=657) or [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670) traits.
  • **Weapon** After you cast a morph or polymorph spell by activating the clay sphere, the weapon becomes malleable, shifting its form to your whims. Your Strikes with the affixed weapon have the [versatile](/Traits.aspx?ID=724) bludgeoning, versatile piercing, and versatile slashing trait for 1 minute. Until the end of your next turn, Strikes with the affixed weapon have their damage dice increase by one step.
**Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You cast [_gouging claw_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1546). Clay Sphere **Source** [Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root](/Sources.aspx?ID=242) pg. 80 **Price** 55 gp Greater Clay Sphere **Source** [Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root](/Sources.aspx?ID=242) pg. 80 **Price** 225 gp --- Resistance when affixed to armor is 5, item bonus when affixed to armor is +2, the spell attack roll is +10, and the spell DC is 20. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_enlarge_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1514). Major Clay Sphere **Source** [Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root](/Sources.aspx?ID=242) pg. 80 **Price** 4,200 gp --- Resistance when affixed to armor is 10, item bonus when affixed to armor is +3, the spell attack roll is +21, and the spell DC is 31. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_cursed metamorphosis_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1479). **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_plant form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1637).]
name: Clay Sphere

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4+"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Necrotic Cap](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3729)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Wardens of Wildwood</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Catalyst" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=396" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root](/Sources.aspx?ID=242) pg. 81

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

You can use this slimy, rotting mushroom as a [spell catalyst](/Rules.aspx?ID=1509) when you cast an [_acid grip_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1436) spell by tapping it against the target, causing the mushroom to release a cloud of necrotic spores. When you do, acid grip loses the [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524) trait, gains the [fungus](/Traits.aspx?ID=614) trait, and all acid damage the spell deals becomes [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) damage. On a hit, the target additionally gains the [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) and [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) conditions, with the listed values, as the spores consume their flesh. As long as the target is taking [persistent void damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), they can’t reduce the value of their sickened condition below 1.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

</row>

</column>

<row gap="medium">

</column>

---

You can use this slimy, rotting mushroom as a [spell catalyst](/Rules.aspx?ID=1509) when you cast an [_acid grip_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1436) spell by tapping it against the target, causing the mushroom to release a cloud of necrotic spores. When you do, acid grip loses the [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524) trait, gains the [fungus](/Traits.aspx?ID=614) trait, and all acid damage the spell deals becomes [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) damage. On a hit, the target additionally gains the [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) and [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) conditions, with the listed values, as the spores consume their flesh. As long as the target is taking [persistent void damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), they can’t reduce the value of their sickened condition below 1.

</title>

<traits>

<trait label="Catalyst" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=396" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root](/Sources.aspx?ID=242) pg. 81

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><row>[Cast a Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734) (add 1 action)</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This palm-sized chunk of wood it rotting, and riddled with mold and fungi. You can crush this wood to use it as a [spell catalyst](/Rules.aspx?ID=1509) when you cast a [_oaken resilience_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1617). When you do, the bark that covers your skin is rotting, and emits a small cloud of spores whenever you're hurt. For the duration, whenever you take physical damage, your rotting bark skin emits a cloud of spores in the listed [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387). Creatures in the area must attempt a Fortitude save at the listed DC to avoid taking the listed poison damage.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><row>[Cast a Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734) (add 1 action)</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This palm-sized chunk of wood it rotting, and riddled with mold and fungi. You can crush this wood to use it as a [spell catalyst](/Rules.aspx?ID=1509) when you cast a [_oaken resilience_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1617). When you do, the bark that covers your skin is rotting, and emits a small cloud of spores whenever you're hurt. For the duration, whenever you take physical damage, your rotting bark skin emits a cloud of spores in the listed [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387). Creatures in the area must attempt a Fortitude save at the listed DC to avoid taking the listed poison damage.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2+"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Inflamation Flask](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3731)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Wardens of Wildwood</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Acid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=3" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Bomb" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=21" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Disease" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=46" />
<trait label="Splash" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=150" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #203 Shepherd of Decay](/Sources.aspx?ID=243) pg. 75

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Strike</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This flask contains a caustic irritant that makes a target’s skin, scales, or carapace extremely sensitive to further nicks and burns. An inflammation flask deals the listed acid damage and acid [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage. On a hit, the target also gains [weakness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2317) to acid, fire, and slashing damage for 3 rounds. Many types of inflammation flask grant a bonus to attack rolls.

The target can end the weakness effect by three means: being drenched with water (such as submersion or exposure to an area [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732) effect), being [Administered First Aid](/Actions.aspx?ID=2396) (whose DC in based on the bomb type), or regaining Hit Points from a single effect equal to twice the inflammation flask’s level. A creature that critically fails to Administer First Aid in this way deals untyped damage to the target equal to the inflammation flask’s initial acid damage.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 2"
pfs="Limited"
>
Lesser Inflammation Flask
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #203 Shepherd of Decay](/Sources.aspx?ID=243) pg. 75

**Price** 5 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The bomb deals 1d6 acid damage and 1 acid splash damage. The target gains weakness 1 to acid, fire, and slashing damage, and the[Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) DC is 17.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Limited"
>
Moderate Inflammation Flask
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #203 Shepherd of Decay](/Sources.aspx?ID=243) pg. 75

**Price** 15 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You gain a +1 item bonus to attack rolls. The bomb deals 2d6 acid damage and 2 acid splash damage. The target gains weakness 2 to acid, fire, and slashing damage, and the Medicine DC is 20.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 12"
pfs="Limited"
>
Greater Inflammation Flask
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #203 Shepherd of Decay](/Sources.aspx?ID=243) pg. 75

**Price** 340 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You gain a +2 item bonus to attack rolls. The bomb deals 3d6 acid damage and 3 acid splash damage. The target gains weakness 3 to acid, fire, and slashing damage, and the Medicine DC is 30.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 18"
pfs="Limited"
>
Major Inflammation Flask
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #203 Shepherd of Decay](/Sources.aspx?ID=243) pg. 75

**Price** 3,750 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You gain a +3 item bonus to attack rolls. The bomb deals 4d6 acid damage and 4 acid splash damage. The target gains weakness 4 to acid, fire, and slashing damage, and the Medicine DC is 38.
name: Inflamation Flask

This flask contains a caustic irritant that makes a target’s skin, scales, or carapace extremely sensitive to further nicks and burns. An inflammation flask deals the listed acid damage and acid [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage. On a hit, the target also gains [weakness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2317) to acid, fire, and slashing damage for 3 rounds. Many types of inflammation flask grant a bonus to attack rolls.

The target can end the weakness effect by three means: being drenched with water (such as submersion or exposure to an area [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732) effect), being [Administered First Aid](/Actions.aspx?ID=2396) (whose DC in based on the bomb type), or regaining Hit Points from a single effect equal to twice the inflammation flask’s level. A creature that critically fails to Administer First Aid in this way deals untyped damage to the target equal to the inflammation flask’s initial acid damage.

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Strike</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The Verduran Forest’s tiny tent-trap spider lays her eggs in a pyramidal web, and her young hatch only in response to intense vibration, such as the struggles of an ensnared insect or even songbird. Sadistic alchemists gather and augment these eggs, packing them in silken satchels that disgorge thousands of biting spider babies on impact. Many types of spider satchel grant a bonus to attack rolls. 

Until shaken off—or for the desperate target, incinerated—these spiders make it difficult to concentrate on anything other than escaping the swarm. The [fascination](/Conditions.aspx?ID=72) effect ends automatically if a creature uses further [hostile action](/Rules.aspx?ID=2251)s against the target, but hostile actions against the target’s allies don’t automatically end the effect.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Burglar's Rosebud](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3733)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Wardens of Wildwood</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Emotion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=60" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
<trait label="Plant" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=125" />
<trait label="Wood" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=508" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #203 Shepherd of Decay](/Sources.aspx?ID=243) pg. 76

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Though originally intended to protect a [green man](/Deities.aspx?ID=221)’s favorite agents against predation by pesky herbivores, thieves have adapted the design to help them disperse guard animals. The soft flower bud belies the horrible perfume contained within. Activated by cracking open the petals, the rosebud exudes a noxious cloud that has the [olfactory](/Traits.aspx?ID=664) trait for 10 minutes. If dropped, it fills a 10-foot-[burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385). If you carry it in one hand and periodically waft it as a free action, the rosebud instead gives you a 10-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387). Creatures that enter or start their turn in the cloud must succeed at a Fortitude save against the listed DC or become [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1. [Animals](/Traits.aspx?ID=531) and [beasts](/Traits.aspx?ID=547) that critically fail are also [fleeing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=74) for 1 round. A creature that successfully saves against the burglar’s rosebud becomes temporarily immune to the effects for 24 hours.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

markdown:
May contain spoilers from Wardens of Wildwood **Source** [Pathfinder #203 Shepherd of Decay](/Sources.aspx?ID=243) pg. 77 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**Strike --- Whereas a durian’s aroma ranges from pleasant to revolting depending on the person, a _durian bomb_ is a fruit that’s been alchemically modified for maximum revulsion. Many types of this bomb grant an item bonus to attack rolls. On a successful [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306), a durian bomb deals the listed piercing damage to the target. Any creature hit by the bomb or in its [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) area must attempt a Fortitude saving throw with a DC based on the bomb’s type. Creatures in the splash area treat the results of their saving throw as one step better. Creatures [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) by a durian bomb are unable to smell anything else while the sickened condition lasts, suppressing any scent special sense they might have. Once a creature is sickened by a _durian bomb_, they’re immune to the sickened condition caused by other durian bombs for 1 minute. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is sickened 1.
**Failure** The creature is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) for 1 round and sickened 1.
**Critical Failure** The creature is dazzled for 1 round and sickened 2. Lesser Durian Bomb **Source** [Pathfinder #203 Shepherd of Decay](/Sources.aspx?ID=243) pg. 77 **Price** 4 gp --- The bomb deals 1d4 piercing damage, and the DC is 15. Moderate Durian Bomb **Source** [Pathfinder #203 Shepherd of Decay](/Sources.aspx?ID=243) pg. 77 **Price** 12 gp --- You gain a +1 item bonus to attack rolls. The bomb deals 2d4 points of piercing damage, and the DC is 17. Greater Durian Bomb **Source** [Pathfinder #203 Shepherd of Decay](/Sources.aspx?ID=243) pg. 77 **Price** 275 gp --- You gain a +2 item bonus to attack rolls. The bomb deals 3d4 points of piercing damage, and the DC is 28. Major Durian Bomb **Source** [Pathfinder #203 Shepherd of Decay](/Sources.aspx?ID=243) pg. 77 **Price** 2,750 gp --- You gain a +3 item bonus to attack rolls. The bomb deals 4d4 points of piercing damage, and the DC is 37.
name: Durian Bomb

markdown:
May contain spoilers from Wardens of Wildwood **Source** [Pathfinder #203 Shepherd of Decay](/Sources.aspx?ID=243) pg. 77 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**Strike --- Whereas a durian’s aroma ranges from pleasant to revolting depending on the person, a _durian bomb_ is a fruit that’s been alchemically modified for maximum revulsion. Many types of this bomb grant an item bonus to attack rolls. On a successful [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306), a durian bomb deals the listed piercing damage to the target. Any creature hit by the bomb or in its [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) area must attempt a Fortitude saving throw with a DC based on the bomb’s type. Creatures in the splash area treat the results of their saving throw as one step better. Creatures [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) by a durian bomb are unable to smell anything else while the sickened condition lasts, suppressing any scent special sense they might have. Once a creature is sickened by a _durian bomb_, they’re immune to the sickened condition caused by other durian bombs for 1 minute. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is sickened 1.
**Failure** The creature is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) for 1 round and sickened 1.
**Critical Failure** The creature is dazzled for 1 round and sickened 2. Lesser Durian Bomb **Source** [Pathfinder #203 Shepherd of Decay](/Sources.aspx?ID=243) pg. 77 **Price** 4 gp --- The bomb deals 1d4 piercing damage, and the DC is 15. Moderate Durian Bomb **Source** [Pathfinder #203 Shepherd of Decay](/Sources.aspx?ID=243) pg. 77 **Price** 12 gp --- You gain a +1 item bonus to attack rolls. The bomb deals 2d4 points of piercing damage, and the DC is 17. Greater Durian Bomb **Source** [Pathfinder #203 Shepherd of Decay](/Sources.aspx?ID=243) pg. 77 **Price** 275 gp --- You gain a +2 item bonus to attack rolls. The bomb deals 3d4 points of piercing damage, and the DC is 28. Major Durian Bomb **Source** [Pathfinder #203 Shepherd of Decay](/Sources.aspx?ID=243) pg. 77 **Price** 2,750 gp --- You gain a +3 item bonus to attack rolls. The bomb deals 4d4 points of piercing damage, and the DC is 37.
name: Major Durian Bomb

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Irritating Seedpod](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3735)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Wardens of Wildwood</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Catalyst" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=396" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #203 Shepherd of Decay](/Sources.aspx?ID=243) pg. 77

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><row>[Cast a Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734) (add 1 action)</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

When you crack open this soft, spongy seedpod, you can use it as a [catalyst](/Rules.aspx?ID=1509) when casting a [_mist_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1606) spell. When you do, irritating pollen fills the area for the spell’s duration. Creatures in the area must attempt a Fortitude saving throw at the listed DC to avoid sneezing uncontrollably. On a failed save, the creature gains the listed condition for the listed time. A creature that succeeds at this saving throw becomes temporarily immune to the irritating seedpod’s pollen for 10 minutes.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

pfs="Limited"

</traits>

**Source** [Pathfinder #203 Shepherd of Decay](/Sources.aspx?ID=243) pg. 77

**Price** 11 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The DC is 15. On a failed save, the creature becomes [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 for 1 round. Rather than last for the duration of the spell, the irritating pollen only remains in the area for 1 round.

</column>

---

The DC is 21. On a failed save, the creature becomes slowed 1 for 1 round.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Tenderizer Grenade](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3736)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Wardens of Wildwood</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Acid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=3" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Bomb" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=21" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Plant" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=125" />
<trait label="Splash" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=150" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #203 Shepherd of Decay](/Sources.aspx?ID=243) pg. 77

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Strike</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Made from the acidic flesh of an especially astringent fruit found on the [Plane of Wood](/Planes.aspx?ID=25), this bomb’s contents soften, oxidize, and season whatever they touch. Many types of this bomb grant an item bonus to attack rolls. 

On a successful Strike, a tenderizer grenade deals the listed acid damage to the target and the listed acid splash damage. Any [resistances](/Rules.aspx?ID=2893) the target has against bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage are reduced by an amount based on the bomb’s type. In addition, the target is showered with appetizing esters, oils, and salts, making them [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) against jaws Strikes, fangs Strikes, or similar Strikes with a creature’s mouth. These additional effects last until the end of your next turn, or for 1 minute on a critical hit.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

markdown:
May contain spoilers from Curtain Call **Source** [Pathfinder #205: Singer, Stalker, Skinsaw Man](/Sources.aspx?ID=246) pg. 85 **Usage** worn collar --- This collar is a silken cord from which hangs a small silver amulet that bears the likeness of a woman with her hand at her throat. A _gasping lament_ is a powerful coda instrument that’s worn rather than held. While you sing, you gain a +2 item bonus to [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) checks to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395) and to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks. Full rules for coda instruments appear [here](/Rules.aspx?ID=1913). **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You expend a number of charges from the collar to cast a spell from its list. **Craft Requirements** Supply one casting of all listed levels of all listed spells. Gasping Lament **Source** [Pathfinder #205: Singer, Stalker, Skinsaw Man](/Sources.aspx?ID=246) pg. 85 **Price** 2,000 gp ---
