markdown:
May contain spoilers from Triumph of the Tusk **Source** [Pathfinder #207: Resurrection Flood](/Sources.aspx?ID=249) pg. 79 **Usage** tattooed on the body --- The [auroch](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3753) depicted by this tattoo is a powerful symbol of strength and resilience. When upgraded, the tattoo expands to depict an increasingly imposing herd of aurochs. **Activate—Aurochs Charge** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) twice and make a melee [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) against a creature within your reach at any point during your movement. If the Strike hits and deals damage, the target attempts a DC 24 Fortitude save to avoid being toppled by the impact. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) until the beginning of its next turn.
**Failure** The target is knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88).
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the target also takes 2d6 bludgeoning damage. Lesser Aurochs' Might Tattoo **Source** [Pathfinder #207: Resurrection Flood](/Sources.aspx?ID=249) pg. 79 **Price** 475 gp Moderate Aurochs' Might Tattoo **Source** [Pathfinder #207: Resurrection Flood](/Sources.aspx?ID=249) pg. 79 **Price** 2,575 gp --- The Fortitude saving throw DC is 30, and the bludgeoning damage dealt by a critical failure is 4d6. Greater Aurochs' Might Tattoo **Source** [Pathfinder #207: Resurrection Flood](/Sources.aspx?ID=249) pg. 79 **Price** 20,000 gp --- The Fortitude saving throw DC is 38, and the bludgeoning damage dealt by a critical failure is 6d6.
name: Aurochs' Might Tattoo

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4+"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Cleft Head Marking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3739)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Triumph of the Tusk</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #207: Resurrection Flood](/Sources.aspx?ID=249) pg. 79

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Caution and misdirection are the hallmarks of your hold. You’ve encountered more travelers than most of Belkzen’s holds ever see, and you need to understand how to deal with them. You’re a proud member of the Cleft Head Hold or have been found worthy of wearing their mark. This tattoo is a crooked line that begins high above one eye and zigzags toward your mouth. The tattoo’s grim appearance can help mask your true motivations. You gain a +1 item bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) checks to [Feint](/Actions.aspx?ID=1434).

**Activate—Unexpected Strike** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) a creature that has the [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) condition with a weapon attack;  **Effect** You deal an extra 1d6 precision damage to the target.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Limited"
>
Lesser Cleft Head Marking
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #207: Resurrection Flood](/Sources.aspx?ID=249) pg. 79

**Price** 80 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 10"
pfs="Limited"
>
Greater Cleft Head Marking
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #207: Resurrection Flood](/Sources.aspx?ID=249) pg. 79

**Price** 850 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus is +2, and Unexpected Strike deals 2d6 precision damage.
name: Cleft Head Marking

The tattoo’s grim appearance can help mask your true motivations. You gain a +1 item bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) checks to [Feint](/Actions.aspx?ID=1434).

**Activate—Unexpected Strike** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) a creature that has the [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) condition with a weapon attack;  **Effect** You deal an extra 1d6 precision damage to the target.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6+"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Stormshard](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3742)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Triumph of the Tusk</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #208: Hoof, Cinder, and Storm](/Sources.aspx?ID=250) pg. 78

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These shards of coalesced necromantic essence are sometimes found in the wake of an ancestor storm. Howling spirits are faintly visible, trapped inside the jagged, dark-green glass.

You can use the shard to cow the [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) or briefly release the tormented souls from inside the glass. While you hold the stormshard, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) checks to [influence](/Rules.aspx?ID=1830) the undead. In addition, you can [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395) undead creatures, even if those creatures are [mindless](/Traits.aspx?ID=652) or otherwise [immune](/Rules.aspx?ID=2313) to [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), or [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) effects.

**Activate—Free the Spirits** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You briefly release the spirits from the _stormshard_ before drawing them back into the glass. Each creature in a 10-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) takes 4d8 [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) damage (DC 20 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2247) Fortitude save). You treat the result of your saving throw as one degree of success better than its outcome.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 12"
pfs="Limited"
>
Greater Stormshard
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #208: Hoof, Cinder, and Storm](/Sources.aspx?ID=250) pg. 79

**Price** 1,800 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus increases to +2. When activating the _stormshard_ to release the spirits, the damage increases to 8d8 (DC 29). In addition, you can choose to increase the radius of the [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) by up to 10 feet.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 18"
pfs="Limited"
>
Major Stormshard
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #208: Hoof, Cinder, and Storm](/Sources.aspx?ID=250) pg. 79

**Price** 19,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus increases to +3. When activating the _stormshard_, the damage increases to 13d8 (DC 38), and you can increase the radius of the emanation by up to 20 feet.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 6"
pfs="Limited"
>
Stormshard
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #208: Hoof, Cinder, and Storm](/Sources.aspx?ID=250) pg. 79

**Price** 230 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This storage container decorated with images of wildlife and berries is popular among Shoanti quahs and other nomadic groups. The box functions like a spacious pouch, holding an amount of Bulk depending on its type, and is inscribed with magic to keep its contents cool to allow for travel with perishable items like meat and fruit. Items in the box are kept fresh for up to a year.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Roc-Shaft Arrow](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3744)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Triumph of the Tusk</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #208: Hoof, Cinder, and Storm](/Sources.aspx?ID=250) pg. 81

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** arrow

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Each of these arrows is made from an immense [roc](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3170)’s flight feather, most of whose vanes have been trimmed to expose the arrow’s shaft. When an activated _roc-shaft arrow_ hits a target, the arrow briefly grows a pair of avian wings and attempts to carry off the target. The target must succeed at a Fortitude save or be moved to a space you choose within an area determined by the arrow’s type; if the target critically fails, the arrow can move them an additional 10 feet. If this would move the target into a [hazardous space](/Rules.aspx?ID=2368), this effect gains the [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631) trait.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 5"
pfs="Limited"
>
Lesser Roc-Shaft Arrow
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #208: Hoof, Cinder, and Storm](/Sources.aspx?ID=250) pg. 81

**Price** 24 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The arrow can move the target 10 feet (20 feet on a critical failure), and the Fortitude save DC is 19.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 10"
pfs="Limited"
>
Moderate Roc-Shaft Arrow
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #208: Hoof, Cinder, and Storm](/Sources.aspx?ID=250) pg. 81

**Price** 160 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The arrow can move the target 15 feet (20 feet on a critical failure), and the Fortitude save DC is 27.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 15"
pfs="Limited"
>
Greater Roc-Shaft Arrow
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #208: Hoof, Cinder, and Storm](/Sources.aspx?ID=250) pg. 81

**Price** 1,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The arrow can move the target 20 feet (30 feet on a critical failure), and the Fortitude save DC is 34.
name: Roc-Shaft Arrow

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7+"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Mat of Resilience](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3745)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Triumph of the Tusk</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #209: Destroyer's Doom](/Sources.aspx?ID=251) pg. 83

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** placed on a surface

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This light but sturdy woven mat, a miniature version of the floating foundations used in villages of the Dirt Sea, is carried tied in a tight roll. When you use an action to unfurl the mat onto an unoccupied horizontal space, the mat covers the existing terrain to create a smooth surface that can be walked on as if it were solid ground. [Difficult](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) or [hazardous terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2368) in that square may be treated as normal while the mat of resilience covers it. The mat can be rolled back up or moved to an adjacent square using an Interact action, but it cannot be moved or put away while a creature is atop it. 

**Activate—Steady Ground** <actions string="Single Action" /> (envision)    **Effect** You unfurl the mat and choose which shape it takes, either a 10-foot square or a 20-foot by 5-foot [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388).

**Activate—Sturdy Foundation** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))   **Requirements** A creature is atop the mat; **Effect** The creature enters a simple stance that makes the most of the mat’s stabilizing magic. While in this stance, if at least half of the creature’s space is atop the mat, it cannot gain the [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) condition.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

level="2"
right="Item 7"
pfs="Limited"
>
Mat of Resilence
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #209: Destroyer's Doom](/Sources.aspx?ID=251) pg. 83

**Price** 330 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 12"
pfs="Limited"
>
Greater Mat of Resilence
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #209: Destroyer's Doom](/Sources.aspx?ID=251) pg. 83

**Price** 1,800 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The mat’s surfaces increase to a 20-foot square and a 40- foot by 5-foot line.
name: Mat of Resilience
